title: CNN This Morning · 2026-07-01 · 民主党初选中的进步派崛起
summary: Dalam pemilihan pendahuluan Partai Demokrat di Colorado, kandidat progresif mengalahkan dua petahana moderat, termasuk Jaksa Agung Negara Bagian Phil Weiser yang mengalahkan Senator Michael Bennet, dan Melat Kiros yang mengalahkan anggota DPR Diana DeGette yang telah menjabat hampir 30 tahun. Analis berpendapat bahwa ini mencerminkan perpecahan generasi dan ideologi di dalam Partai Demokrat, serta ketidakpuasan terhadap kebijakan Israel, politik uang, dan isu-isu lainnya. Sementara itu, Mahkamah Agung mempertahankan larangan atlet transgender, sekutu Trump terus menantang hak kewarganegaraan berdasarkan kelahiran. Kebakaran hutan melanda Barat, LeBron James menjadi free agent.

paragraphs.speaker: Brad Smith
paragraphs.en: That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah akhir dari CNN Headline News. Saya Brad Smith. Acara CNN This Morning dengan Otie Cornish dimulai sekarang.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Today in the group chat, do Democrats see progressives as a problem? Another Democratic socialist knocks out a moderate. Is this something establishment Dems need to start worrying about? MELAT KIROS (D),
paragraphs.cn: Topik obrolan hari ini: Apakah Demokrat menganggap progresif sebagai masalah? Seorang sosialis demokrat lainnya mengalahkan seorang moderat. Apakah ini sesuatu yang perlu dikhawatirkan oleh Demokrat mapan? Melat Kiros (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Congressional Candidate
paragraphs.en: From coast to coast, from every level of office, we are taking back our party and our country.
paragraphs.cn: Dari pantai timur ke pantai barat, dari semua tingkat jabatan publik, kami merebut kembali partai dan negara kami.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: A rare rebuke from the conservative court. Birthright citizenship upheld. How the president wants to get his way anyway. And Western wildfires explode, scorching thousands of acres. The short relief before even more dangerous weather later this week. And LeBron James, a free agent. And almost every NBA team is sure to want him. Some Lakers fans aren't so sad to see him go.
paragraphs.cn: Teguran langka dari pengadilan konservatif. Hak kewarganegaraan berdasarkan kelahiran dipertahankan. Presiden tetap ingin mencapai tujuannya dengan cara apa pun. Kebakaran hutan melanda Barat, membakar ribuan hektar. Istirahat singkat sebelum cuaca yang lebih berbahaya akhir pekan ini. LeBron James menjadi free agent. Hampir setiap tim NBA pasti menginginkannya. Beberapa penggemar Lakers tidak terlalu sedih dengan kepergiannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: In Lakers' standards, it was -- it was a bust. It was a waste of money.
paragraphs.cn: Menurut standar Lakers, ini -- ini adalah kegagalan. Ini pemborosan uang.

paragraphs.speaker: Kiros
paragraphs.en: They said the establishment and the oligarchy is just too big and too powerful to overcome. You are the proof that the power of organized people beats the power of organized money.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka mengatakan kemapanan dan oligarki terlalu besar dan kuat untuk dikalahkan. Anda membuktikan bahwa kekuatan rakyat yang terorganisir mengalahkan kekuatan uang yang terorganisir.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: A warning shot for establishment Dems. Two longtime prominent Democrats are now out. Good morning, everybody. And here's where we begin. Stunning losses in Colorado. State attorney general Phil Weiser upsets Senator Michael Bennet in the Democratic primary for governor. PHIL WEISER (D),
paragraphs.cn: Peringatan bagi Demokrat mapan. Dua Demokrat yang sudah lama dikenal kini tersingkir. Selamat pagi semuanya. Kita mulai dari sini. Kekalahan mengejutkan di Colorado. Jaksa Agung Negara Bagian Phil Weiser mengalahkan Senator Michael Bennet dalam pemilihan pendahuluan gubernur Demokrat. Phil Weiser (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: State Attorney General
paragraphs.en: People want to know that they're electing someone who's in this work to fight for them, who they know cares about them. And the goal of our campaign was to break through. We broke through a lot of negative ads. We broke through a lot of noise, because I believe in the power of people.
paragraphs.cn: Orang-orang ingin tahu bahwa mereka memilih seseorang yang berjuang untuk mereka, yang peduli pada mereka. Tujuan kampanye kami adalah untuk menembus. Kami menembus banyak iklan negatif. Kami menembus banyak kebisingan, karena saya percaya pada kekuatan rakyat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And then you had Melat Kiros beat a nearly 30-year incumbent congresswoman, Diana DeGette, last night. Kiros, the latest Democratic socialist to win their primary.
paragraphs.cn: Dan kemudian tadi malam Melat Kiros mengalahkan anggota DPR petahana Diana DeGette yang telah menjabat hampir 30 tahun. Kiros adalah sosialis demokrat terbaru yang memenangkan pemilihan pendahuluan.

paragraphs.speaker: Kiros
paragraphs.en: We believe that fundamental change can and will happen if we fight for it; if we organize and show no fear in standing up for what's right. That is the message that Denver has sent to both parties, to Donald Trump, and to the entire country.
paragraphs.cn: Kami percaya bahwa jika kami berjuang untuk itu, jika kami terorganisir, membela keadilan tanpa rasa takut, perubahan mendasar bisa dan akan terjadi. Ini adalah pesan dari Denver kepada kedua partai, kepada Donald Trump, dan kepada seluruh negeri.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, today in the group chat, Zolan Kanno-Youngs, CNN political analyst and White House correspondent with "The New York Times"; Terry Schilling, president of the American Principles Project; and Antjuan Seawright, Democratic strategist. Zolan, I want to start with you, because back a few months ago when there was infighting in the DNC itself, there were all these people that were like, I'm taking my toys and going home. Right? Like David Hogg, I'm going to go spend my own money, just as Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, dalam obrolan hari ini, ada analis politik CNN, koresponden Gedung Putih New York Times Zolan Kanno-Youngs; ketua American Principles Project Terry Schilling; dan ahli strategi Demokrat Antwan Sirait. Zolan, saya ingin mulai dengan Anda, karena beberapa bulan lalu, ketika ada pertikaian internal di Komite Nasional Demokrat, banyak orang seperti berkata, saya akan mengambil mainan saya dan pulang. Kan? Seperti David Hogg, saya akan menghabiskan uang saya sendiri, seperti Demokrat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We're going to recruit our own people. There was this real sense that there was going to be money spent elsewhere by different recruiters for different reasons. And I want to show the viewers right now how well justice Democrats are doing, as it turns out, primary winners. I think I'm looking at a count of up to eight faces here. What are they doing differently than the DNC?
paragraphs.cn: Kami akan merekrut orang kami sendiri. Saat itu memang ada perasaan bahwa perekrut yang berbeda akan membelanjakan uang di tempat lain karena alasan yang berbeda. Saya ingin menunjukkan kepada pemirsa seberapa baik kinerja Justice Democrats sekarang, ternyata mereka menang dalam pemilihan pendahuluan. Saya pikir saya menghitung, ada sebanyak delapan wajah di sini. Apa perbedaan pendekatan mereka dengan Komite Nasional Demokrat?

paragraphs.speaker: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: Yes. It's interesting, because that fight over spending, I do think, represented a broader fight over the direction of the Democratic Party --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, ini menarik, karena saya pikir pertarungan tentang pengeluaran ini mewakili pertarungan yang lebih luas tentang arah Partai Demokrat --

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: -- and who should lead them. And what we're seeing with this series of wins, the ones you just outlined, but also were coming a week after Mamdani's --
paragraphs.cn: -- -- dan siapa yang harus memimpin mereka. Rangkaian kemenangan yang kita lihat ini, seperti yang baru saja Anda sebutkan, dan itu terjadi setelah Mamdani --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And those are in --
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Itu terjadi di --

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: -- sweep in New York.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- seminggu setelah kemenangan besar di New York.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- Colorado, New Jersey, California.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- Colorado, New Jersey, California.

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: Right, right. Across the country. I do think the themes in these races speak to -- they resonate with something broader in the Democratic base across the country. Right? In the last presidential campaign, I would often hear from Democratic voters. They didn't feel people represented them. You just highlighted somebody who won a generational race, right?
paragraphs.cn: Ya, ya. Di seluruh negeri. Saya memang berpikir bahwa tema dalam kampanye ini beresonansi dengan sesuatu yang lebih luas di kalangan pemilih basis Demokrat nasional. Kan? Dalam kampanye presiden terakhir, saya sering mendengar pemilih Demokrat berkata bahwa mereka merasa tidak ada yang mewakili mereka. Anda baru saja menyebut seseorang memenangkan kampanye antargenerasi, kan?

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: She was born a year after the incumbent won her first race.
paragraphs.cn: Dia lahir setahun setelah petahana memenangkan kampanye pertamanya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But with the views that come with that, in some cases.
paragraphs.cn: Tetapi dalam beberapa kasus, juga disertai dengan pandangan yang menyertainya.

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: And that brings -- brings us to Israel, as well.
paragraphs.cn: Ini juga membawa kita ke Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: We have seen Israel. And the issue of whether the Democratic Party will support U.S. support for Israel, that has resonated both in New York and these races.
paragraphs.cn: Kita melihat situasi di Israel. Pertanyaan apakah Demokrat akan mendukung dukungan AS terhadap Israel bergema di New York dan dalam kampanye ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. I think I'm also fascinated by that, because what happened with Kamala Harris and Biden is they truly struggled to talk about Israel and Gaza when that fight was at its height. Then you have someone like Melat Kiros. I want to play one more thing from her, this Colorado congressional candidate who had a win, because she uses an argument that you wouldn't have heard from the Biden administration last fall.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Saya juga tertarik dengan ini, karena Kamala Harris dan Biden memang kesulitan membicarakan Israel dan Gaza pada puncak konflik. Lalu muncul orang seperti Melat Kiros. Saya ingin memutar lagi klipnya, kandidat DPR dari Colorado ini menang karena dia menggunakan argumen yang tidak akan diucapkan oleh pemerintahan Biden musim gugur lalu.

paragraphs.speaker: Kiros
paragraphs.en: When I wrote a letter defending students' rights to protest the genocide in Gaza, my law firm -- my law firm told me, Take it down or you're fired. I didn't flinch. We will not wait to put an end to the politics of the past; to get big money out of our politics; and to reject corporate PACs and AIPAC. And no, we will not wait to end the genocide in Palestine.
paragraphs.cn: Ketika saya menulis surat untuk membela hak mahasiswa memprotes genosida di Gaza, firma hukum saya mengatakan, tarik surat itu atau Anda dipecat. Saya tidak mundur. Kami tidak akan menunggu untuk mengakhiri politik masa lalu; tidak akan menunggu untuk mengeluarkan uang besar dari politik; tidak akan menunggu untuk menolak PAC perusahaan dan AIPAC. Tidak, kami tidak akan menunggu untuk mengakhiri genosida di Palestina.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, every key word that's a problem for Democrats and a problem, especially, for people who are concerned about the rhetoric around Israel, punctuated by an actual air horn. I want to play one more thing. A voter, and then, Antjuan, I want you to talk out of this.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, setiap kata kunci yang menjadi masalah bagi Partai Demokrat, terutama bagi mereka yang peduli dengan retorika seputar Israel, dipotong oleh suara sirene yang nyata. Saya ingin memutar klip lain. Seorang pemilih, lalu Antwan, saya ingin Anda menanggapinya.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: I like to see that she has more socialist ideals.
paragraphs.cn: Saya suka melihatnya memiliki lebih banyak cita-cita sosialis.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I think people are tired of our tax dollars being spent overseas and on bombs to kill children.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir orang-orang bosan dengan uang pajak kami dihabiskan di luar negeri dan digunakan untuk membuat bom yang membunuh anak-anak.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I'm anti-AIPAC, and so I just wanted to vote for someone that was standing up against Israel. And I knew that the incumbent was for Israel. So voted against her.
paragraphs.cn: Saya menentang AIPAC, jadi saya hanya ingin memilih seseorang yang menentang Israel. Saya tahu petahana mendukung Israel, jadi saya memilih menentangnya.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: People like Kiros and the DSA in general are presenting more, like, compelling arguments about how to make this country better for everyone.
paragraphs.cn: Orang-orang seperti Quiara dan DSA secara umum mengajukan argumen yang lebih meyakinkan tentang bagaimana membuat negara ini lebih baik bagi semua orang.

paragraphs.speaker: Antjuan Seawright
paragraphs.en: A couple of things. We're having a generational conversation and an ideological conversation in these primaries. I think the common denominator is that people are frustrated. People are pissed off. And we collective Democrats are against Donald Trump and the Republican majority. How we talk about these issues has defined these primaries. And -- But I will make the continued argument that the most important thing for Democrats to remember is 218 is the name of the game.
paragraphs.cn: Ada beberapa poin. Dalam pemilihan pendahuluan ini, kita sedang melakukan dialog antargenerasi dan dialog ideologis. Saya pikir kesamaannya adalah orang-orang merasa frustrasi. Orang-orang marah. Kita sebagai Demokrat bersama-sama menentang Donald Trump dan mayoritas Partai Republik. Bagaimana kita membicarakan isu-isu ini mendefinisikan pemilihan pendahuluan ini. Tapi saya akan terus berargumen bahwa hal terpenting yang perlu diingat Demokrat adalah 218 adalah nama permainannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Antjuan Seawright
paragraphs.en: You can talk boldly and loudly about all of these policies, but if you cannot govern, if you do not win, and losers do not legislate. And when you talk about the way that some of our more progressive candidates who are winning in deep-blue districts, the issues they are talking about are going to require them to have to compromise on what they believe if they want to respond to what they campaign.
paragraphs.cn: Anda bisa berbicara lantang dan berani tentang semua kebijakan ini, tetapi jika Anda tidak bisa memerintah, jika Anda tidak bisa menang, pecundang tidak bisa membuat undang-undang. Ketika Anda berbicara tentang beberapa kandidat yang lebih progresif yang menang di distrik yang sangat biru, isu-isu yang mereka bicarakan akan menuntut mereka untuk berkompromi dengan keyakinan mereka sendiri ketika mereka ingin memenuhi janji kampanye.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Require them or require you and the rest of the establishment, because that's where the money is going to have to come from, probably?
paragraphs.cn: Menuntut mereka atau menuntut Anda dan anggota kemapanan lainnya, karena dana mungkin harus datang dari sana?

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Well, I don't consider myself establishment. I consider myself just a Democrat. I think we've put these labels on folks, and I think that has been part of the somewhat division you see within the primaries. At the end of the day, when you go into the United States Congress, you're labeled as a Democrat versus Republican. We should anchor that there. But the second point is what we're seeing across the board. I think this is probably true in Republican primaries.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, saya tidak menganggap diri saya sebagai kemapanan. Saya hanya menganggap diri saya seorang Demokrat. Saya pikir kita memberi orang label ini, dan itu sebagian dari alasan mengapa Anda melihat semacam perpecahan dalam pemilihan pendahuluan. Pada akhirnya, ketika Anda masuk ke Kongres AS, Anda diberi label Demokrat atau Republik. Kita harus berlabuh pada itu. Tapi poin kedua adalah apa yang kita lihat di mana-mana. Saya pikir ini mungkin juga terjadi di pemilihan pendahuluan Partai Republik.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: It's easier to vote against something than it is to vote for something. So, people are voting for who are -- who has been in charge --
paragraphs.cn: Memilih menentang sesuatu lebih mudah daripada memilih mendukung sesuatu. Jadi, orang memilih mereka yang -- -- yang telah berkuasa -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: And who they feel like a leader.
paragraphs.cn: dan siapa yang mereka rasa sebagai pemimpin.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Terry, can I have the answer to that, especially to this point? I'm looking at like, for instance, the "Post" response to the Supreme Court upholding bans on trans athletes. This kind of follows what he's saying. It's easy to say no. "No" is easy to put in a headline. It's easy to put it in ad.
paragraphs.cn: Terry, bolehkah saya menjawab pertanyaan itu, terutama mengenai poin itu? Saya memikirkan, misalnya, tanggapan The Washington Post terhadap keputusan Mahkamah Agung yang menegakkan larangan atlet transgender. Itu mirip dengan apa yang dia katakan. Mengatakan 'tidak' itu mudah. 'Tidak' mudah menjadi berita utama dan mudah dimasukkan ke dalam iklan.

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.en: No, that's exactly right, Audie. And I think that candidates like this, that are more radical, where the mask has come off, they're winning in deep blue districts, right? Zohran Mamdani is not running in Virginia. He's running in New York City.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, benar sekali, Audi. Saya pikir kandidat yang lebih radikal seperti ini, ketika topengnya dibuka, mereka menang di distrik yang sangat biru, kan? Zoran Mamdani tidak mencalonkan diri di Virginia. Dia mencalonkan diri di New York City.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: And the same thing here. It's going to be interesting to see how these new, more radically left-wing candidates fare in terms of the -- the upcoming midterms. But it's also going to be interesting to see how they change the coalition for the Democratic Party. When President Trump came on the scene back, you know, ten years ago, he changed the makeup of the Republican Party.
paragraphs.cn: Hal yang sama. Akan menarik untuk melihat bagaimana kandidat sayap kiri baru yang lebih radikal ini akan tampil dalam pemilihan paruh waktu mendatang. Tapi sama menariknya adalah bagaimana mereka akan mengubah koalisi Partai Demokrat. Ketika Presiden Trump muncul sekitar sepuluh tahun yang lalu, dia mengubah komposisi Partai Republik.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: And I could see a lot of these higher propensity voters --
paragraphs.cn: Saya bisa melihat banyak pemilih dengan kecenderungan tinggi ini -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can I play an example?
paragraphs.cn: Boleh saya beri contoh?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Here's an example of someone who thrives in the Trump era. The Colorado candidate for governor on the Republican side. These are the kinds of questions and back and forth he's dealing. I just want to play this for the audience before we go.
paragraphs.cn: Ini contoh seseorang yang berkembang di era Trump. Calon gubernur Partai Republik dari Colorado. Dia menghadapi jenis pertanyaan dan perdebatan ini. Sebelum acara berakhir, saya ingin penonton mendengar ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Your campaign website falsely claimed that you had rescued more than 45,000 women and children. You claim that you once called in a U.S. military airstrike that killed 70 ISIS fighters. You perform exorcisms, commanding demons to come out of people. Your claim that your abusive stepfather forced you to kill a man when you were 7 years old, is that the only person you've ever killed? VICTOR MARX (R),

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Candidate For Governor
paragraphs.en: Well, I would say as a -- as a child, yes.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, saya ingin mengatakan, sebagai seorang anak, ya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It feels like the takeaway for Republicans has not been radicals, don't need them.
paragraphs.cn: Sepertinya pelajaran yang didapat Partai Republik bukanlah radikal, tidak membutuhkan mereka.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It seems like it's, like, certain people are more than encouraged by the current environment.
paragraphs.cn: Tampaknya, beberapa orang justru didorong oleh lingkungan saat ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: I would -- I would -- I would ignore people like this.
paragraphs.cn: Saya akan -- -- Saya akan -- -- Saya akan mengabaikan orang seperti itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Why? He's running for governor.
paragraphs.cn: Kenapa? Dia mencalonkan diri sebagai gubernur.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: No, I know, but I'm from Illinois. Right? And so -- so what happens in these deep blue states where, like, Republicans basically give up on is it's not --
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, saya tahu, tapi saya dari Illinois. Kan? Jadi, di negara bagian yang sangat biru ini, di mana Partai Republik pada dasarnya menyerah, apa yang terjadi bukan -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Which is not Colorado, by the way. It is not deep blue, but go on.
paragraphs.cn: 顺便说一句，科罗拉多州不是深蓝州。但请继续。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Colorado is pretty blue, Audie. But I look, I would -- I would look more to the states like Virginia and New Jersey.
paragraphs.cn: 科罗拉多州相当蓝，奥黛。但我会更关注像弗吉尼亚州和新泽西州这样的州。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But then why run a guy like that?
paragraphs.cn: 但既然如此，为什么还要推这样的人参选？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: We -- well, look, because a lot of the serious people are not running in Colorado. If I was a serious candidate for GOP governor --
paragraphs.cn: 我们 -- -- 嗯，你看，因为很多严肃的人并没有在科罗拉多州参选。如果我是共和党州长的严肃候选人 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: Not just run a guy, but he's leading in donations in that race, as well.
paragraphs.cn: 不只是推人参选，而且他在那场竞选中收到的捐款也领先。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: there's money donors, probably not from Colorado, are giving him money.
paragraphs.cn: 有捐款人，可能不是来自科罗拉多州，在给他钱。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: And what -- and what I will say is that the -- the -- the people, the actual voters, they feel like both establishments are screwing them over. They feel like they're getting behind further and further. And that's the Democrats and the Republicans. And the first candidate that actually addresses these concerns in a real way is going to be the winner.
paragraphs.cn: 我要说的是 -- -- 选民们，真正的选民，他们觉得两党都在欺骗他们。他们觉得自己越来越落后。无论是民主党还是共和党。第一个真正解决这些问题的候选人将会获胜。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right. Well, sorry, this one is really -- I'm befuddled. So, I want to know more, especially -- well, because where the money goes is where they think there is money well spent. I want to come back to the Supreme Court, though. They have rejected the president's efforts to end birthright citizenship. But the White House isn't giving up. They're going to try to come up with some other way to bring this back.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。抱歉，这个真的让我很困惑。所以我想了解更多，尤其是 -- -- 因为钱流向哪里，就说明他们认为钱花得值。不过，我想回到最高法院的话题。他们驳回了总统终止出生公民权的努力。但白宫没有放弃。他们打算想出其他办法来重新提出这个问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And then we want to talk about the children in Venezuela who have been pulled alive from the rubble after that earthquake. One other thing. The queen is back. Serena Williams returns to Wimbledon, her first singles match in four years. She did lose to Australian Maya Joint, who spoke after the match.
paragraphs.cn: 然后我们想谈谈委内瑞拉地震后从废墟中救出的孩子们。还有一件事。女王回来了。塞雷娜·威廉姆斯重返温布尔登，这是她四年来首场单打比赛。她输给了澳大利亚选手玛雅·乔因特，后者在赛后发表了讲话。

paragraphs.speaker: Maya Joint
paragraphs.en: She has such an aura. She's such a legend, and this court has so many huge names that have played on it. I just -- yes, I was -- I've been dreaming about this moment since I was a little kid.
paragraphs.cn: 她气场太强大了。她是个传奇，这片球场上有过太多大牌球员。我只是 -- -- 是的，我 -- -- 我从小就一直梦想着这一刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, the Supreme Court now on summer vacation, wrapping up their latest term Tuesday after handing down 58 opinions, including a dozen major rulings. Among those final rulings, a 6-to-3 decision which struck down President Trump's effort to end birthright citizenship via an executive order. The president's allies are already vowing to reverse the decision. REP. MIKE JOHNSON (R-LA): It certainly is time for us to do everything that's possible. I don't know what that is.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，最高法院现在放暑假了，周二结束了最新一个任期，共发布了58份意见书，其中包括十几项重大裁决。在最后一批裁决中，有一项6比3的裁决，推翻了特朗普总统通过行政命令终止出生公民权的努力。总统的盟友已经誓言要推翻这一裁决。共和党众议员迈克·约翰逊：当然是时候尽一切可能了。我不知道那是什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I will tell you that it's a big concern for the American people, because it's been abused.
paragraphs.cn: 我要告诉你，这是美国人民非常关心的问题，因为它被滥用了。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.en: We have to keep fighting, Laura, because we actually have an opportunity to reverse this decision, just as we've reversed so many bad decisions throughout the generations.
paragraphs.cn: 我们必须继续战斗，劳拉，因为我们实际上有机会推翻这一决定，就像我们在几代人以来推翻了许多糟糕的决定一样。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Joining the group chat, Steve Vladeck, CNN Supreme Court analyst and professor at the Georgetown University Law Center. I want to talk about these two hot-button ones. First, birthright citizenship and also upholding the ban on transgender youth in sports. What is it exactly that the court was arguing about in the end on this birthright citizenship, because it was actually closer than some legal watchers thought it would be?
paragraphs.cn: 好的。加入群聊的是史蒂夫·弗拉德克，CNN最高法院分析师和乔治城大学法律中心教授。我想谈谈这两个热点问题。首先是出生公民权，以及维持对跨性别青少年参与体育运动的禁令。最高法院在出生公民权问题上最终争论的到底是什么？因为实际上它比一些法律观察人士预期的要接近。

paragraphs.speaker: Steve Vladeck
paragraphs.en: It was, Audie. I mean, so there are two different things going on in the birthright citizenship case. It's a six-justice majority that holds that the president's executive order itself is unlawful. And so, it's Chief Justice Roberts. It's Justice Amy Coney Barrett, Justice Brett Kavanaugh, and the three Democratic appointees. But Kavanaugh actually broke from the majority on the constitutional question: whether Congress might actually be able to change the rules for birthright citizenship.
paragraphs.cn: 确实如此，奥迪。我的意思是，出生公民权案件中有两件不同的事情。六位大法官的多数意见认为总统的行政命令本身是非法的。所以，首席大法官罗伯茨、大法官艾米·科尼·巴雷特、布雷特·卡瓦诺以及三位民主党任命的大法官。但卡瓦诺实际上在宪法问题上与多数意见分道扬镳：国会是否真的能够改变出生公民权的规则。

paragraphs.speaker: Steve Vladeck
paragraphs.en: On that question, an interpretation of the 14th Amendment, it was only 5 to 4, which I think was a lot closer than folks expected, not just before this case really got to the Supreme Court, but even after the oral argument back in April. Now, 5-4 is still not a majority. And so that doesn't mean --
paragraphs.cn: 在那个问题上，对第十四修正案的解释，只有5比4，我认为这比人们预期的要接近得多，不仅是在这个案件真正到达最高法院之前，甚至在四月份的口头辩论之后也是如此。现在，5比4仍然不是多数。所以这并不意味着 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But it's opening a door. If you're a lawmaker or somebody who's really into this, you're like, well, what's that? You know, is that language I can use? I just want to show where the polling is on this. "New York Times" reporting 55 percent of Americans support birthright citizenship. And then they mentioned this, that underneath the surface, there's a division between MAGA-identified Republicans and -- am I allowed to say establishment Republicans? Maybe? Antjuan?
paragraphs.cn: 但这打开了一扇门。如果你是一名立法者或对此非常感兴趣的人，你会想，嗯，那是什么？你知道，那是我可以使用的语言吗？我只想展示一下民意调查的结果。《纽约时报》报道55%的美国人支持出生公民权。然后他们提到，在表面之下，MAGA认同的共和党人和 -- -- 我可以说建制派共和党人吗？也许？安特万？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. And -- and wanting to push this forward. And I'm kind of curious about that. Is this something you campaign on or is this --
paragraphs.cn: 好的。并且 -- -- 想要推动这一点。我对此有点好奇。这是你竞选时主张的问题，还是 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Well, look, I think there's an existential crisis with the Republican Party and with America right now in terms of immigration. We should be able to determine who can become American citizens. And I think that, ultimately, at the Supreme Court we've been dealing with is the highest levels of lawfare. The Supreme Court will hold up our laws and our chain (ph). Trump -- Trump has struggled to be able to fire the people in his own cabinet, in his own administration, who don't want to do the job he's doing.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，听着，我认为共和党和美国目前在移民问题上存在生存危机。我们应该能够决定谁可以成为美国公民。而且我认为，最终，我们在最高法院处理的是最高级别的法律战。最高法院将维护我们的法律和我们的链条。特朗普 -- -- 特朗普一直难以解雇他自己内阁和政府中那些不想做他工作的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can I come to this? The Overton Window has moved -- a Washington phrase I despise. But it is true. We went from being like, yes, you're born here, you're a citizen, to now what was once a fringe legal thing --
paragraphs.cn: 我能插一句吗？奥弗顿窗口已经移动了 -- -- 一个我讨厌的华盛顿短语。但这是真的。我们从"是的，你出生在这里，你就是公民"变成了现在曾经是边缘法律问题的事情 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: Is one vote away from being constitutional law.
paragraphs.cn: 离成为宪法法律只差一票。

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: I will say, though, the administration, when it comes to preparation for this issue, has mainly put the focus on messaging on it. I asked Pam Bondi at a press conference after an earlier Supreme Court hearing on this issue. President Trump and her appeared at the White House press briefing, and I asked them, what -- how will this actually work? Who will be verifying citizenship?
paragraphs.cn: 不过我要说，政府在准备这个问题时，主要把重点放在了信息传递上。在早些时候最高法院就这个问题举行听证会后的一次新闻发布会上，我向帕姆·邦迪提问。特朗普总统和她出现在白宫新闻发布会上，我问他们，这实际上将如何运作？谁来核实公民身份？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And we should be clear: the executive order would have said at least one parent has to be a citizen --
paragraphs.cn: 我们应该明确：行政令会规定，至少父母一方必须是公民 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- in order for a child to be legally considered.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 孩子才能被合法考虑。

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: Or at least someone with -- with some kind of permanent immigration status.
paragraphs.cn: Or at least someone with some sort of permanent immigration status.

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: Right? And I think what --
paragraphs.cn: Right? I think -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right. Well, hold on one second. I want to stop with that one. Not because it's not interesting, but because we have two others that are far more controversial. And one of them is striking down the ban on transgender youth in sports. This, I want to play for you a piece of tape about how the lawyers for this are thinking about the road ahead. Specifically, these are the athletes who applaud the ban and the lawyer who argued for them.
paragraphs.cn: Okay, hold on a second. I want to pause here. Not because this isn't interesting, but because we have two more controversial topics. One is overturning the ban on transgender youth participating in sports. I want to play you a recording of how lawyers see the path forward. Specifically, these are the athletes who support the ban and the lawyers defending them.

paragraphs.speaker: Madison Kenyon
paragraphs.en: My heart is full of gratitude for the attorney generals and for ADF and all their work on this, and to know that no female in the 27 states that have the protection will have to compete against a male like I did.
paragraphs.cn: I am grateful to the state attorneys general, ADF, and all the work they have done. Knowing that in the 27 states with protections, no woman will have to compete against men like I did brings me comfort.

paragraphs.speaker: Kristen Waggoner
paragraphs.en: I do expect this decision eventually to impact the 23 states, because the court recognized that there are physical advantages that can exist, and we're already litigating in those states.
paragraphs.cn: I do expect this ruling to eventually affect the other 23 states, because the courts recognize there are biological advantages, and we have already filed lawsuits in those states.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Donald Trump on social media calling this a big win. Can you briefly touch on what Kristen Waggoner, the attorney there -- she's from the Alliance Defending Freedom -- is saying? Is she saying the 23 states right now who allow transgender youth in sports better watch out, because there is legal language in here that can be used against them?
paragraphs.cn: Donald Trump called this a big victory on social media. Can you briefly talk about what lawyer Christine Wagner -- -- from the Alliance Defending Freedom -- -- is saying? Is she saying that the 23 states currently allowing transgender youth in sports should watch out, because the legal language here can be used against them?

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: So, I mean, I think she's saying that. I'm not sure she's right, which is to say, you know, the Supreme Court yesterday said it's not unconstitutional for states like West Virginia and Idaho to have a ban. But there's really nothing in there that says it would be unconstitutional to not.
paragraphs.cn: So, I think she is saying that. But I'm not sure she's correct, meaning, you know, the Supreme Court ruled yesterday that it is not unconstitutional for states like West Virginia and Idaho to implement bans. But there is nothing in it that says not implementing a ban is unconstitutional.

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: And so, I think what we're going to see for now, Audie, is that this issue really is something that's going to vary state by state until and unless you see, again, some kind of national legislation from Congress, which, you know, I don't think is something we'll hear any time soon.
paragraphs.cn: So, I think what we will see now, Ode, is that this issue will really vary by state, until and unless you see some kind of national legislation from Congress again, which I don't think will happen anytime soon.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: If the bans are upheld, do you still make ads about it? Or is this a win, and you're done?
paragraphs.cn: If the ban is upheld, will you still advertise on this? Or is this a victory and you stop?

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Yes. No, you have to make ads about it, because the goal of the Democratic Party is still to put gender identity into national and federal civil rights law. And if they keep pushing forward, that's what's going to happen all across the country. Those 27 states that worked very hard to protect girls' sports, those laws are all going to be wiped out. And boys will be able to compete in girls' sports wherever they want to.
paragraphs.cn: Yes. No, you have to advertise on it, because the Democrats' goal is still to include gender identity in national and federal civil rights laws. If they push forward, this will happen nationwide. The 27 states that worked to protect women's sports, those laws will all be overturned. Boys will be able to compete in girls' sports anytime, anywhere.

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: I'm not sure how the laws are going to be wiped out when the Supreme Court just said yesterday it doesn't violate federal anti- discrimination law.
paragraphs.cn: I'm not sure how those laws will be overturned, because the Supreme Court just ruled yesterday that it does not violate federal anti-discrimination law.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Well, because federal civil rights law trumps state -- any state law.
paragraphs.cn: Well, because federal civil rights law supersedes state law -- -- any state law.

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: Right. And one of those laws was at issue in yesterday's case, and the Supreme Court specifically said -- in fact, on this point, the court was actually unanimous that Title IX, the federal anti-discrimination law from 1972, is not inconsistent with these bans. So, you know, I mean, I understand as a matter of politics, this is going to be something that gets everyone's dander up. I think yesterday's decision really does, perhaps even more so than Dobbs in the abortion context, return this question to the states.
paragraphs.cn: Exactly. And the case yesterday involved one of those laws, and the Supreme Court specifically noted -- -- in fact, on this point, the court was actually unanimous -- that the 1972 federal anti-discrimination law Title IX does not conflict with these bans. So, you know, I mean, from a political standpoint, this will be something that riles everyone up. I think yesterday's ruling really, perhaps even more than Dobbs on abortion, sends this issue back to the states.

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: The states are going to have different views on what the answer is.
paragraphs.cn: States will have different answers.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: I think, broadly speaking, all of these court decisions are going to become political issues both in the fall and elections to come. When you talk about this fundamental truth, when it comes to a person being an American, being born in this country, being an American citizen, I think the Republicans are going to use that as an opportunity to make immigration a red-meat issue in the next couple of months, and a presidential election year. The same thing with the transgender or the girls and boys in sports.
paragraphs.cn: I think, overall, all these court rulings will become political issues in the fall and future elections. When you talk about this basic fact -- -- a person as an American, born in this country, becoming a U.S. citizen -- -- I think Republicans will use this opportunity to turn immigration into a provocative issue in the coming months and the presidential election year. The same goes for transgender or men's and women's sports.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: And I think also, the campaign finance issue. I think all of these things are going to become campaign issues that both parties campaign on as a -- as a way to galvanize, energize their voters.
paragraphs.cn: And I think there's also the campaign finance issue. I think all of these will become campaign issues, and both parties will use them as a way to mobilize and motivate voters.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can I ask you that one last thing about that campaign finance bill? Because none of these issues matter if the money flowing in and out of politics can swing things one way or another without the voters' consent, effectively.
paragraphs.cn: Can I ask you one last question about the campaign finance bill? Because if the money flowing in and out of politics can sway things without voter consent, then all these issues are irrelevant.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, what happened with this ruling?
paragraphs.cn: So, what was the outcome of this ruling?

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: I mean, this was the third ruling yesterday. It's gotten the least attention in some respects. It actually might be the most important in the short term. Because what the Supreme Court held is that the federal limits that go back to the 1970s on what are called coordinated expenditures, when a candidate coordinates their expenditures with the national party, those limits have been in place since 1976.
paragraphs.cn: I mean, this is the third ruling yesterday. In some ways, it got the least attention. Actually, in the short term, it might be the most important. Because the Supreme Court ruled that federal limits on so-called coordinated spending since the 1970s -- -- limits when candidates coordinate spending with national parties -- -- these limits have been in place since 1976.

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: And the Supreme Court struck them down yesterday, even though, Audie, in 2001, the Supreme Court had specifically upheld them. What I think that really does is it opens the spigot in this cycle, especially for Republicans. But going forward, right, for individual candidates to basically draw from national pools of money without any constraint.
paragraphs.cn: And the Supreme Court overturned those limits yesterday, even though in 2001, the Supreme Court explicitly upheld them. I think this actually opens the floodgates for money in this election, especially for Republicans. But looking ahead, individual candidates can basically draw unlimited funds from the national pool.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: I was just about to say, this is a huge win for Republicans. Perhaps this is the most consequential decision by the court with campaign finance since Citizens United.
paragraphs.cn: I was just about to say, this is a huge victory for Republicans. This might be the most impactful ruling on campaign finance since Citizens United.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And we are going to have --
paragraphs.cn: We will -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Republicans are going to benefit.
paragraphs.cn: Republicans will benefit.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- the conversation with that. I want to save time, because we have the head of the DCCC, meaning we have someone from the establishment whose job is to, like, raise that money.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- have a conversation about this. I want to save time, because we have the chair of the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee, meaning we have someone from the establishment whose job is to raise these funds.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: From the Democratic Party. Not the establishment.
paragraphs.cn: Dari Partai Demokrat, bukan dari kalangan establishment.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- and share, from the Democratic Party, also known as the establishment Democrats. Steve, thank you so much for explaining it.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- dan berbagi, dari Partai Demokrat, juga disebut Demokrat establishment. Steve, terima kasih banyak atas penjelasannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.en: Thanks for having me.
paragraphs.cn: Terima kasih atas undangannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You survived the group chat. You're going to get a mug. You can take that with you. All right. So today, we're going to talk about this ahead. President Trump testing out his new Qatari jet. And then LeBron James leaves the Lakers. Where is he going to go? Even politicians are trying to sway the star. And we're going to go to break with this view over the sunrise at Virginia beach.
paragraphs.cn: Kamu berhasil melewati obrolan grup. Kamu akan mendapatkan cangkir. Kamu bisa membawanya. Baiklah, hari ini kita akan membahas ini lebih awal. Presiden Trump menguji pesawat barunya dari Qatar. Lalu LeBron James meninggalkan Lakers. Ke mana dia akan pergi? Bahkan para politisi mencoba memengaruhi bintang ini. Kita akan memasuki iklan dengan pemandangan matahari terbit di Virginia Beach.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's now 28 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. We want to go back to Venezuela and tell you what's going on with the search-and-rescue teams there. Because after those two deadly earthquakes, the death toll has climbed to more than 1,900 people. However, there is still reason for hope.
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang pukul 28 menit lewat. Ini adalah ringkasan berita pagimu. Kita kembali ke Venezuela, memberitahumu situasi tim pencari dan penyelamat di sana. Karena setelah dua gempa mematikan itu, jumlah korban tewas telah meningkat menjadi lebih dari 1900 orang. Namun, masih ada alasan untuk berharap.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: (SPEAKING FOREIGN LANGUAGE)
paragraphs.cn: (Berbicara dalam bahasa asing)

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: New video showing rescue crews pulling a 3-year-old boy from the rubble of a collapsed building. He had been buried alive for six days. And today, a temporary Medicare program will begin coverage for weight loss medication. Millions will be able to receive GLP-1s for just $50 a month if they meet the right criteria. The program is expected to run through the end of 2027. And LeBron James leaving the L.A. Lakers. He does not plan to retire. He wants to be a free agent, according to sources.
paragraphs.cn: Video baru menunjukkan petugas penyelamat mengeluarkan seorang anak laki-laki berusia 3 tahun dari reruntuhan bangunan yang runtuh. Dia terkubur hidup-hidup selama enam hari. Hari ini, program asuransi kesehatan sementara akan mulai mencakup obat penurun berat badan. Jika memenuhi syarat, jutaan orang hanya perlu membayar $50 per bulan untuk mendapatkan obat GLP-1. Program ini diperkirakan akan berlangsung hingga akhir tahun 2027. LeBron James meninggalkan Los Angeles Lakers. Dia tidak berniat pensiun. Menurut sumber, dia ingin menjadi free agent.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The rumors say he may return to Cleveland, but that's not confirmed. James's departure is leaving fans with mixed emotions.
paragraphs.cn: Ada rumor bahwa dia mungkin kembali ke Cleveland, tetapi ini belum dikonfirmasi. Kepergian James membuat para penggemar memiliki perasaan campur aduk.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I was hoping he'd stay in L.A., but the fact that he's going somewhere else is pretty cool. But LeBron is one of the greatest. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Awalnya aku berharap dia tetap di Los Angeles, tapi kalau dia pergi ke tempat lain juga keren. Tapi LeBron adalah salah satu pemain terhebat.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Departure is leaving fans with mixed emotions.
paragraphs.cn: Kepergiannya membuat para penggemar memiliki perasaan campur aduk.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I was hoping he'd stay in L.A. But the fact that he's going somewhere else is pretty cool. But LeBron's one of the greatest.
paragraphs.cn: Awalnya aku berharap dia tetap di Los Angeles. Tapi kalau dia pergi ke tempat lain juga keren. Tapi LeBron adalah salah satu pemain terhebat.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I mean it was nice having him. And I think he should just retire.
paragraphs.cn: Maksudku, ada dia itu bagus. Tapi aku pikir dia harus pensiun.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I mean he won a championship. It was in a bubble, but he won. So, I guess -- I guess it would be all right. But in Lakers standards, it was -- it was a bust.
paragraphs.cn: Maksudku, dia memenangkan satu gelar juara. Meskipun di dalam gelembung, dia menang. Jadi, kurasa -- kurasa itu tidak buruk. Tapi menurut standar Lakers, ini -- ini adalah kegagalan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Almost every team in the NBA is sure to be circling. Even politicians getting in on it. Minnesota Governor Tim Walz posting this, trying to sway him to the Timberwolves. And straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, another traditional Democrat is out. Does the party have a bigger problem on its hands? The chair of the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee, Congresswoman Suzan DelBene, joins the group chat next. And tonight, the U.S. faces Bosnia and Herzegovina for its first knockout game in the World Cup.
paragraphs.cn: Hampir setiap tim NBA pasti memperhatikan. Bahkan politisi ikut campur. Gubernur Minnesota Tim Walz memposting untuk mencoba meyakinkannya bergabung dengan Timberwolves. Selanjutnya di CNN This Morning, seorang Demokrat tradisional lainnya tersingkir. Apakah partai menghadapi masalah yang lebih besar? Ketua Komite Kampanye Kongres Demokrat, Perwakilan Suzan DelBene, akan bergabung dalam obrolan grup. Malam ini, tim AS akan bermain di pertandingan pertama babak gugur Piala Dunia melawan Bosnia dan Herzegovina.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Whoever wins this match will make it to round 16. The U.S. is hoping to make it to their first quarterfinal since 2002.
paragraphs.cn: Siapa pun yang memenangkan pertandingan ini akan melaju ke 16 besar. AS berharap untuk mencapai perempat final untuk pertama kalinya sejak 2002.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, the socialist wave alive and well in the Democratic Party. Last night's Colorado Democratic primary reveals 29- year-old Democratic socialist Melat Kiros defeated 15-term incumbent Diana DeGette. Kiros is a lawyer, PhD student, who has not run for office before. She was born in 1997. That is the same year DeGette took office. MELAT KIROS (D),

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Congressional Candidate
paragraphs.en: Experience only matters if you actually do something with it. And we're finding now, with a lot of incumbent Democrats, that they're not actually able to deliver meaningfully on the things that working families need right now.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, whether traditional Democrats, as Antjuan says, wants to admit it or not, we're seeing a trend. In New York, Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three Democratic socialists in June primaries. They all won. In L.A., progressive city council member Nithya Raman forced a November runoff against incumbent Mayor Karen Bass. Here in Washington, D.C., Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George has advanced as the top contender for mayor.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And so, we have here Suzan DelBene, Democratic congresswoman, joining the chat because she chairs the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee. It is your job to get to 218, right, this fall? REP. SUZAN DELBENE (D-WA): Two hundred and eighteen in the House.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Two hundred and eighteen in the House. So, one of the reasons why I wanted to talk to you today, we talked earlier in the show about how different factions of Democrats were saying, we're going to spend. We're going to put behind people in various primaries. The Justice Democratic group was one of them. And they now have had at least seven wins. And those wins have been in California, Colorado, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania. I'm going to put aside philosophical differences.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: What does it mean when an outside group has had these kinds of gains and forced incumbents off -- out of the spot?

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: Well, let's be clear, the types of districts we're talking about, bright blue districts across the country, whereas the path to the majority is through purple districts across the country. That's really been our focus.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, you're not worried about the general in those places?

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: The path to the majority, none of the --

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Delbene
paragraphs.en: You know, what we've seen changes the math to get to the majority. We need to keep purple seats that we have and flip seats that Republicans have to get to 218. We have 71 districts on our map, 45 where we're on offense that we think we can flip. That's the math to get the majority. And frankly, in these districts, in these swing districts, we have incredible candidates running because they are connected to their communities and focused on really appealing across their communities.
paragraphs.cn: Kau tahu, situasi yang kita lihat ini mengubah perhitungan untuk meraih mayoritas. Kita perlu mempertahankan daerah pemilihan ungu yang kita miliki, dan membalikkan kursi yang dipegang Partai Republik untuk mencapai 218 kursi. Peta kita memiliki 71 daerah pemilihan, 45 di antaranya adalah target ofensif yang kita yakini bisa dibalik. Itulah perhitungan untuk meraih mayoritas. Terus terang, di daerah-daerah ini, di daerah pemilihan yang tidak pasti ini, kita memiliki kandidat yang sangat baik, karena mereka terhubung erat dengan komunitas dan fokus pada hal-hal yang benar-benar menarik dukungan seluruh komunitas.

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Delbene
paragraphs.en: These are communities with differing political views but it's about bringing people together.
paragraphs.cn: Komunitas-komunitas ini memiliki pandangan politik yang berbeda, tetapi kuncinya adalah menyatukan orang-orang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. So, are there any lessons to be drawn, though, from their successes? Are there any lessons to be drawn, even from, let's say, Colorado last night?
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Lalu, apakah ada pelajaran yang bisa diambil dari kesuksesan mereka? Bahkan dari Colorado tadi malam, apakah ada pelajaran yang bisa diambil?

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: Well, there are absolutely lessons to be drawn, which is, have candidates who are deeply connected to their communities, focused on the issues that matter, and really are authentic, independent- minded voices for their communities, who are coming to Congress to get things done. We actually won last cycle, in 2024, 14 districts that Donald Trump also won. Fourteen Democrats won because we had strong candidates who spoke for people in their communities across ideological bounds.
paragraphs.cn: Tentu saja ada pelajaran yang bisa diambil, yaitu memiliki kandidat yang terhubung erat dengan komunitas, fokus pada isu-isu penting, benar-benar menyuarakan suara yang autentik dan independen untuk komunitas, mereka datang ke Kongres untuk menyelesaikan sesuatu. Faktanya, pada siklus pemilu sebelumnya tahun 2024, kami memenangkan 14 daerah pemilihan yang juga dimenangkan oleh Donald Trump. 14 Demokrat menang karena kami memiliki kandidat yang kuat, yang melintasi batas-batas ideologis, dan menyuarakan kepentingan rakyat di komunitas mereka.

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: And that, I think, is going to be critical to taking back the majority, including in Colorado.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir ini sangat penting untuk merebut kembali mayoritas, termasuk di Colorado.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let me have you answered to some of the critics then. This is Dan Pfeiffer, co-host of "Pod Save America." I think people kind of tend to think of these guys as Obama bros. But here's what they're saying when they see this outside energy. DAN PFEIFFER, CO-HOST, "POD SAVE AMERICA": They have captured the energy. They are being more creative. They're being more strategic. They're being more aggressive.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, biarkan aku mendengar tanggapanmu terhadap beberapa kritikus. Ini Dan Pfeiffer, co-host dari "Pod Save America". Saya pikir orang cenderung menganggap orang-orang ini sebagai pendukung Obama. Tapi inilah yang mereka katakan ketika mereka melihat energi eksternal ini. Dan Pfeiffer, co-host "Pod Save America": Mereka menangkap energi itu. Mereka lebih kreatif. Mereka lebih strategis. Mereka lebih agresif.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And if you are someone in the Democratic establishment, you should be deeply concerned about what this says about where the party is, what the standing of the party is, what the ability of the party infrastructure is to actually win elections.
paragraphs.cn: Jika kamu bagian dari establishment Demokrat, kamu harus sangat khawatir tentang apa yang dikatakan ini tentang keadaan partai, posisi partai, dan kemampuan infrastruktur partai untuk benar-benar memenangkan pemilu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can you respond to that? It's very specific.
paragraphs.cn: Bisakah kamu menanggapi? Ini spesifik.

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: Well, I think it really comes down to having candidates that -- who I feel -- you know, communities feel like are speaking for them. And in these swing districts across the country, we have incredible candidates, farmers, veterans, mayors.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, saya pikir ini kembali lagi pada memiliki kandidat -- saya rasa -- kau tahu, komunitas merasa mereka menyuarakan diri mereka sendiri. Di daerah pemilihan yang tidak pasti di seluruh negeri ini, kami memiliki kandidat yang sangat baik, ada petani, veteran, walikota.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He says you should be deeply concerned about what this says. And he talks about the ability of party infrastructure to actually win. He's not talking about what you're talking about, right? Like, do the locals connect? He's saying, can you guys help out your people compared to the outsiders who have captured some kind of energy?
paragraphs.cn: Dia mengatakan Anda harus sangat memperhatikan apa artinya ini. Dia berbicara tentang kemampuan infrastruktur partai untuk benar-benar memenangkan pemilu. Dia tidak membicarakan apa yang Anda katakan, kan? Misalnya, hubungan masyarakat lokal dengan komunitas? Dia mengatakan, dibandingkan dengan orang luar yang menangkap energi tertentu, dapatkah Anda membantu orang Anda sendiri?

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: Absolutely. We gained seats last cycle, ran ahead of the top of the ticket, and are on a strong path across the country to pick up seats. Great candidates. I can't emphasize how important that is to have great candidates across the country, and they are running ahead of Republicans. They have -- they are incredible. I said, you know, farmers, mayors, veterans --
paragraphs.cn: Tentu. Kami memenangkan kursi di siklus sebelumnya, unggul dari kandidat presiden, dan memiliki momentum kuat secara nasional untuk menambah kursi. Kandidat yang hebat. Saya tidak bisa cukup menekankan betapa pentingnya memiliki kandidat hebat di seluruh negeri, dan mereka unggul dari Partai Republik. Mereka -- mereka sangat luar biasa. Saya katakan, Anda tahu, petani, walikota, veteran --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: Including in Colorado, who also won in that primary last night in Colorado Five, Jessica Killin, a veteran, a mom, a strong voice for her community, going to flip a seat in Colorado Springs area. And Manny Rutinel, who won the primary in Colorado Eight, running against a Republican, Gabe Evans, who has been the example, kind of the poster child of what people are frustrated with Republicans, cutting health care, cutting food and nutrition.
paragraphs.cn: Termasuk di Colorado, Jessica Killin yang memenangkan pemilihan pendahuluan di Distrik 5 Colorado tadi malam, dia seorang veteran, seorang ibu, juru bicara komunitas yang kuat, dan akan membalikkan kursi di daerah Colorado Springs. Dan Manny Rutinel, yang memenangkan pemilihan pendahuluan di Distrik 8 Colorado, akan bersaing dengan anggota Partai Republik Gabe Evans, yang selalu menjadi contoh khas ketidakpuasan terhadap Partai Republik, seperti memotong perawatan kesehatan, memotong makanan dan nutrisi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So then let me talk money. Let me talk money. NRCC, right, your corollary, right now they have raised, at least according to CBS News, 164 million plus, DCCC, $139 million. When I look at the Supreme Court ruling yesterday, which says that there is going to be the ability to coordinate between candidates and party infrastructure on money, there are some people, Zolan help me out, what's the question here? Does it look good for Democrats given --
paragraphs.cn: Jadi biarkan saya bicara tentang dana. Biarkan saya bicara tentang dana. NRCC, kan, mitra Anda, saat ini mereka telah mengumpulkan setidaknya $164 juta, menurut CBS News, sementara DCCC mengumpulkan $139 juta. Ketika saya melihat keputusan Mahkamah Agung kemarin yang mengizinkan koordinasi dana antara kandidat dan infrastruktur partai, beberapa orang, Zoran tolong bantu saya, apa masalahnya? Apakah ini menguntungkan bagi Partai Demokrat, mengingat --

paragraphs.speaker: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: On -- when it comes to the war chest that they have? Some -- no. I mean you've got -- the Republicans have been able to amass massive donations, already signaling that after the Supreme Court decision you're going to see a flood towards something like the Ken Paxton race, right, as well as looking ahead to the general. I think of all those MAGA inc. dinners that have occurred in West Palm Beach as well. That's a little separate. But when it comes to that money difference right now, they do have an edge.
paragraphs.cn: Tentang -- dana kampanye yang mereka miliki? Tidak. Maksud saya, Partai Republik telah mampu mengumpulkan banyak sumbangan, telah menunjukkan bahwa setelah keputusan Mahkamah Agung, Anda akan melihat dana mengalir ke kampanye seperti Ken Paxton, dan melihat ke pemilihan umum. Saya memikirkan semua makan malam MAGA Inc. di West Palm Beach. Ini agak berbeda. Tapi dalam hal kesenjangan dana saat ini, mereka memang memiliki keunggulan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can I let her answer this because you're -- this is your job.
paragraphs.cn: Bisakah saya biarkan dia menjawab pertanyaan ini, karena ini tugas Anda.

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: Well, first of all, you talked about MAGA Inc. It turns out corruption helps folks raise money there. And I think that the reason Republicans wanted to see this change in campaign finance law is because they know that our candidates are stronger financially than their candidates.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, pertama, Anda menyebut MAGA Inc. Ternyata, korupsi membantu mereka mengumpulkan dana. Saya pikir alasan Partai Republik ingin melihat perubahan undang-undang keuangan kampanye adalah karena mereka tahu kandidat kami lebih kuat secara finansial daripada kandidat mereka.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But now that they have it, what do you do? I mean, Platner, right now, has seen his -- and he's running for Senate in Maine against Susan Collins, he has actually seen his lead diminish for a variety of reasons. But some of those reasons are being outspent according to their own campaign three to one in just the last two weeks.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi sekarang mereka punya ini, apa yang Anda lakukan? Maksud saya, Platter, dia sekarang mencalonkan diri sebagai senator di Maine, menantang Susan Collins, sebenarnya keunggulannya menyusut karena berbagai alasan. Tapi beberapa alasan itu adalah dalam dua minggu terakhir, menurut tim kampanye mereka sendiri, mereka kalah dalam pengeluaran tiga banding satu oleh lawan.

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: So, we don't need more. We need enough. We have incredible candidates. We are focused on the issues that matter. Families are struggling across the country with high costs. Republicans are indifferent. They don't care that the policies they put in place are hurting working families across the country. We are right on the issues. We have incredible candidates. We need to make sure we have the resources to get the message out, and we will. Republicans are focused on resource because they got nothing else out there.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, kami tidak perlu lebih banyak. Kami perlu cukup. Kami memiliki kandidat hebat. Kami fokus pada isu-isu penting. Keluarga di seluruh negeri berjuang dengan biaya tinggi. Partai Republik tidak peduli. Mereka tidak peduli bahwa kebijakan yang mereka terapkan menyakiti keluarga pekerja di seluruh negeri. Kami benar dalam masalah. Kami memiliki kandidat hebat. Kami perlu memastikan ada sumber daya untuk menyampaikan pesan, dan kami akan melakukannya. Partai Republik fokus pada sumber daya karena mereka tidak punya yang lain.

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: People know that. We still are going to take back the majority all across the country, great candidates, and we'll have the resources to --
paragraphs.cn: Orang-orang tahu itu. Kami masih akan merebut kembali mayoritas di seluruh negeri, kandidat hebat, dan kami akan memiliki sumber daya --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Well, one -- I want to let Antjuan jump in because his group chats are blowing up as we are sitting here.
paragraphs.cn: Baik. Yah, saya ingin Antwan ikut campur, karena grup chat-nya sudah meledak saat kita duduk di sini.

paragraphs.speaker: Antjuan Seawright
paragraphs.en: It would not be uncommon for us to be outspent in any given election. Republicans have more millionaires and billionaires who --
paragraphs.cn: Dalam pemilu mana pun, tidak jarang pengeluaran kami dikalahkan oleh lawan. Partai Republik memiliki lebih banyak jutawan dan miliarder, dan mereka --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Believe that no political romance without finance. That wouldn't be uncommon. Where our strength has always lied has been with people and the money -- momentum not necessarily money. And in this conversation we're having with -- this growing pains conversation we're having within our party with these primaries, look, I think growth is good.
paragraphs.cn: Percaya tidak ada romantisme politik tanpa uang. Ini tidak jarang. Keunggulan kami selalu terletak pada rakyat dan dana -- momentum tidak selalu sama dengan uang. Dalam percakapan yang sedang kami lakukan ini -- percakapan tentang sakit tumbuh dari pemilihan pendahuluan ini -- saya pikir pertumbuhan itu baik.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: The real test for some of these candidates who do not consider themselves a part of the Democratic traditional infrastructure is going to be how they continue to galvanize or energize these voters for us to win up and down the ballot in November.
paragraphs.cn: Bagi kandidat yang tidak menganggap diri mereka sebagai bagian dari infrastruktur tradisional Partai Demokrat, ujian sebenarnya adalah bagaimana mereka terus menginspirasi atau memobilisasi pemilih ini, sehingga kita memenangkan suara dari atas ke bawah dalam pemilu November.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: We encourage and we --
paragraphs.cn: Kami mendorong dan kami --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But does that mean, Terry, for people like you, you're there writing, like spend here, force them to spend here, force them to spend here. Like, what is the counter?
paragraphs.cn: Tapi apakah ini berarti, Terry, orang seperti Anda, Anda menulis di sana, seperti menghabiskan uang di sini, memaksa mereka menghabiskan uang di sana, memaksa mereka menghabiskan uang di sana. Lalu, apa tindakan balasannya?

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.en: Look -- well, look, there's been a lot of talk about the infrastructure and the process problems that the Democratic Parties have. I think the real problem is actually policy. The Democratic Party, on economics, their messaging used to be so good, but then you had this horrible four years with Joe Biden where people saw 9.5 percent inflation.
paragraphs.cn: Dengar -- yah, dengar, sudah banyak diskusi tentang infrastruktur dan proses Partai Demokrat. Saya pikir masalah sebenarnya adalah kebijakan. Pesan ekonomi Partai Demokrat dulu bagus, tapi selama empat tahun buruk pemerintahan Joe Biden, orang-orang melihat inflasi 9,5%.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Real quick. But at the same time, if Democrats could just moderate on men and women's sports, right?
paragraphs.cn: Bicara cepat. Tapi sementara itu, jika Partai Demokrat bisa mengambil sikap moderat dalam masalah olahraga pria dan wanita, kan?

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: If they could moderate on this crazy trans stuff, you would make my life a lot harder and we wouldn't be able to run as hard a campaign ads. But they're too radical in the culture war.
paragraphs.cn: Jika mereka bisa mengambil sikap moderat dalam masalah transgender yang gila ini, hari-hari saya akan jauh lebih sulit, dan kami tidak bisa memasang iklan kampanye yang sekeras itu. Tapi mereka terlalu radikal dalam perang budaya.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: But, Terry, Terry --
paragraphs.cn: Tapi, Terry, Terry --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: And every one of these socialists that's running, they're going to have to -- they're going to force all the people in the purple districts and swing districts to answer to them. James Talarico, is he really going to bring more Democrats across the finish line?
paragraphs.cn: Dan setiap sosialis yang mencalonkan diri, mereka harus -- mereka akan memaksa semua orang di daerah ungu dan daerah ayunan untuk menanggapi mereka. Apakah James Talarico benar-benar bisa membawa lebih banyak Demokrat melewati garis finis?

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Terry. Terry, Terry, Terry, Terry --
paragraphs.cn: Terry. Terry, Terry, Terry, Terry --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let me -- let me let Antjuan -- but, Antjuan, to his point, he's the guy who makes these kind of ads we're talking about. So --
paragraphs.cn: Biarkan saya -- biarkan saya biarkan Antwan -- tapi, Antwan, soal pendapatnya, dia adalah orang yang membuat iklan yang kita bicarakan ini. Jadi --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Yes, they don't mind trying to distract us with these unnecessary issues. But --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, mereka tidak keberatan mengalihkan perhatian kita dengan masalah yang tidak perlu ini. Tapi --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: I don't think the culture is a distraction.
paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak menganggap budaya sebagai gangguan.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Let me finish. The bottom line is, the affordability crisis, brought to you and sponsored by Donald Trump --
paragraphs.cn: Biarkan saya selesai. Pada akhirnya, krisis keterjangkauan yang dibawa dan disponsori oleh Donald Trump --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: And Joe Biden before him.
paragraphs.cn: dan Joe Biden sebelumnya.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Donald Trump, MAGA Mike, and the Republican majority is going to be your liability going into the midterms. Every single issue that you guys campaigned on in the 2024 election, you're upside down on, including the economy. You cannot run away from the fact that more Americans are paying more for gas, goods, groceries, services and health care, including the 18.5 million Americans in places that Donald Trump won who have seen their premiums, co-pays and deductibles rise because of your lack of action.
paragraphs.cn: Donald Trump, MAGA Mike, dan mayoritas Partai Republik akan menjadi beban Anda dalam pemilihan paruh waktu. Dalam setiap isu yang Anda kampanyekan pada pemilu 2024, Anda berada dalam posisi yang kurang menguntungkan, termasuk ekonomi. Anda tidak bisa lari dari kenyataan bahwa lebih banyak orang Amerika membayar lebih untuk bensin, barang, bahan makanan, layanan, dan perawatan kesehatan, termasuk 18,5 juta orang Amerika di daerah yang dimenangkan Donald Trump, yang premi, copay, dan deductible-nya naik karena kelambanan Anda.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I just want to bring it --
paragraphs.cn: Saya hanya ingin menyebutkan --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: And it's the one-year anniversary --
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah peringatan satu tahun --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: Of the big, ugly bill. Republicans across the board voting to slash healthcare, slash nutrition --
paragraphs.cn: RUU yang besar dan jelek itu. Partai Republik secara bulat memilih untuk memotong perawatan kesehatan, memotong nutrisi --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: Basic economic issues.
paragraphs.cn: Masalah ekonomi dasar.

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: You talk about economic issues.
paragraphs.cn: Anda berbicara tentang masalah ekonomi.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: So, the goal is to raise taxes on most Americans. That's what that bill primarily did.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, tujuannya adalah menaikkan pajak bagi kebanyakan orang Amerika. RUU itu terutama melakukan hal itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Ok, let me -- let me pause --
paragraphs.cn: Oke, biarkan saya -- biarkan saya jeda --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: No, go across the country. Go across --
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, berkeliling negeri. Berkeliling --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You guys, hold on one second. Let me pause. To your point, I understand and I hear what you're saying, but we are also going to have a segment about a frozen Congress where the president won't even let them sign their housing affordability bill in front of everyone. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: Semuanya, tunggu sebentar. Biarkan saya jeda. Tentang poin Anda, saya mengerti dan mendengar maksud Anda, tetapi kami juga akan memiliki segmen tentang pembekuan Kongres, di mana presiden bahkan tidak membiarkan mereka menandatangani RUU keterjangkauan perumahan di depan semua orang. (Percakapan silang)

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, so that affordability messaging is on a wobbly roller coaster.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, jadi pesan tentang keterjangkauan itu seperti naik roller coaster yang goyah.

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: They know they're losing. They know they're losing.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka tahu mereka kalah. Mereka tahu mereka kalah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But for you guys, I think what I'm trying to understand is, do you end up pulling these folks closer, capturing that energy, or do things get bogged down in this battle about who's under what umbrella?
paragraphs.cn: Tetapi bagi Anda, saya pikir saya mencoba memahami, apakah Anda pada akhirnya akan menarik para pemilih ini lebih dekat, menangkap energi itu, atau hal-hal akan berubah menjadi pertengkaran tentang siapa yang berada di bawah payung siapa?

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: There is incredible energy around the candidates we have running in these purple districts across the country. I encourage you to go out -- go out to Colorado Five and see Jessica Killin and the work that she is doing to flip a seat there, and how much momentum she's built in her district. Or Manny Rutinel, who is the candidate in Colorado Eight. Go to Arizona and Joe Mendoza in Tucson area, who's also going to flip a seat in (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: Kami memiliki energi yang luar biasa di sekitar kandidat kami yang mencalonkan diri di distrik-distrik ungu ini di seluruh negeri. Saya mendorong Anda untuk melihat -- pergi ke Distrik Kelima Colorado, lihat Jessica Killin dan pekerjaannya untuk membalikkan kursi itu, dan seberapa banyak momentum yang telah dia kumpulkan di distriknya. Atau Manny Rutinel, yang merupakan kandidat di Distrik Kedelapan Colorado. Pergi ke Joe Mendoza di daerah Tucson, Arizona, yang juga akan membalikkan kursi (tidak jelas).

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I hear the ads to come. But, yes.
paragraphs.cn: Saya mendengar iklan yang akan datang. Tapi, ya.

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: They are -- these are folks who are incredible candidates and want to come and make a difference for their communities versus be a rubber stamp for Donald Trump.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka -- orang-orang ini adalah kandidat yang sangat baik yang ingin membawa perubahan bagi komunitas mereka, bukan menjadi stempel karet Donald Trump.

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.en: That's all Republicans are right now.
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang semua anggota Partai Republik seperti itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Congresswoman, thank you so much for being here. It's not quite a rubber stamp. We're going to talk about that later, since you all are headed home for recess right now. But we do want to turn to this. Apparently, there are 250 presidential pardons for 250 years. Trump wants to have one of the largest clemency actions in modern history. And then, of course, it's a love story. Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding celebration expected to take place at Madison Square Garden in New York City this week.
paragraphs.cn: Anggota Dewan Perwakilan, terima kasih banyak telah datang ke sini. Ini tidak sepenuhnya stempel karet. Kami akan membahasnya nanti, karena Anda sekarang akan pulang untuk reses. Tapi kami benar-benar ingin beralih ke topik ini. Jelas, ada 250 pengampunan presiden dalam 250 tahun. Trump ingin mengambil salah satu tindakan pengampunan terbesar dalam sejarah modern. Dan kemudian, tentu saja, ada kisah cinta. Perayaan pernikahan Taylor Swift dan Travis Kelce dijadwalkan minggu ini di Madison Square Garden, New York City.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Sources telling CNN a rehearsal will happen tomorrow. Then the wedding is set for Friday. And then Tuesday CNN saw workers at MSG wearing a Taylor Swift carpenter's t-shirt. Was he just trolling? Is this confirmation? Either way, respect.
paragraphs.cn: Sumber memberi tahu CNN bahwa latihan akan dilakukan besok. Lalu pernikahan dijadwalkan pada hari Jumat. Pada hari Selasa, CNN melihat staf Madison Square Garden mengenakan kaos Taylor Swift bertuliskan 'Carpenter'. Apakah dia hanya bercanda? Apakah ini konfirmasi? Bagaimanapun, hormat.

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: I have a lot of people have asked me for pardons. I call him Puff Daddy has asked me for a pardon. Well, I'm allowed to give her a pardon, but I -- nobody's approached me with it. Nobody's asked me about it.
paragraphs.cn: Banyak orang meminta pengampunan kepada saya. Orang yang saya sebut Diddy meminta pengampunan kepada saya. Hmm, saya bisa memberinya pengampunan, tapi saya -- tidak ada yang mengajukan permintaan kepada saya. Tidak ada yang bertanya kepada saya tentang hal itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: President Trump has not been shy about using his executive power to grant pardons since returning to office. He may be getting ready to issue hundreds more. Now, according to a new report in "The Atlantic," the Trump administration is debating a plan to issue 250 pardons for America's 250th birthday. Who might be on the list? Well, joining us now in the group chat, Sarah Fitzpatrick, who's the author of that story.
paragraphs.cn: Presiden Trump, sejak kembali menjabat, tidak segan-segan menggunakan kekuasaan eksekutif untuk memberikan pengampunan. Dia mungkin bersiap untuk mengeluarkan ratusan pengampunan lagi. Sekarang, menurut laporan baru dari The Atlantic, pemerintahan Trump sedang mendiskusikan rencana untuk mengeluarkan 250 pengampunan untuk merayakan ulang tahun ke-250 Amerika. Siapa yang mungkin masuk dalam daftar? Nah, bergabung dengan kami dalam obrolan grup sekarang adalah Sarah Fitzpatrick, penulis laporan itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Sarah, can you give me some names? Who are they batting around? SARAH FITZPATRICK, STAFF WRITER, "THE ATLANTIC": So, there's quite a large name -- group of names that's being discussed, but there are some incredibly controversial names that have come up that even the lobbyists, even the people who are making these deals, feel very uncomfortable with. For example, the Malaysian fugitive Jho Low, who is credited with laundering billions and billions of dollars.
paragraphs.cn: Sarah, bisakah Anda menyebutkan beberapa nama? Siapa saja yang mereka pertimbangkan? Kontributor The Atlantic, Sarah Fitzpatrick: Jadi, ada banyak nama yang dibahas, tetapi beberapa nama yang sangat kontroversial muncul, bahkan membuat para pelobi dan orang yang melakukan transaksi ini merasa sangat tidak nyaman.
