Misalnya, buronan Malaysia, Low Taek Jho, yang diduga mencuci miliaran dolar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The rapper Pras, who was involved in an illegal influence campaign of Trump officials. And then we've seen even such a discussion, a very serious, high-level discussion of a pardon for the founder of the OneTaste orgasmic meditation cult. And so, we're seeing a kind of -- a mix of people who can afford to go after these pardons, as opposed to the traditional people that you would see pardons who truly are doing it on merit.
paragraphs.cn: Rapper Pras, yang terlibat dalam kampanye pengaruh ilegal terhadap pejabat Trump. Lalu kita bahkan melihat diskusi serius tingkat tinggi tentang pengampunan bagi pendiri kultus meditasi orgasme OneTaste. Jadi, kita melihat campuran orang-orang yang mampu membayar untuk mengejar pengampunan ini, bukan orang-orang yang biasanya Anda lihat mendapatkan pengampunan berdasarkan prestasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.en: And I think that's what's making everyone very concerned. The other thing is there's -- on the list, I'm told, is quite a few foreign entities, people that are very rich, very well-connected from other countries. And so we're --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir itulah yang membuat semua orang sangat khawatir. Hal lain yang saya dengar adalah bahwa ada cukup banyak entitas asing dalam daftar, orang-orang yang sangat kaya dan memiliki koneksi luas dari negara lain. Jadi kami --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Middle Eastern countries or --
paragraphs.cn: Negara-negara Timur Tengah, atau --

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.en: A combination. Europe, Middle East, Asia. And so, I think that's giving everyone a lot of pause about, why are we considering, you know, this is an American -- a truly American clemency option. Why are we seeing these people on the list?
paragraphs.cn: Campuran. Eropa, Timur Tengah, Asia. Jadi, saya pikir ini membuat banyak orang berhenti dan berpikir, mengapa kita mempertimbangkan -- Anda tahu, ini adalah opsi pengampunan Amerika yang sesungguhnya. Mengapa kita melihat orang-orang ini dalam daftar?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right. Because if you're going to message it -- first of all, it's messaging at its base.
paragraphs.cn: Tepat. Karena jika Anda ingin mengirim pesan -- pertama-tama, ini pada dasarnya adalah pengiriman pesan.

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.en: One hundred percent.
paragraphs.cn: Seratus persen.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's 250th anniversary, 250 people. It's designed to be talked about. And so, you're saying there are people saying, if we want to talk about it, why would we pick kind of people who are seen as the worst of the worst, and also people who are not Americans or from other places.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah peringatan 250 tahun, 250 orang. Ini dirancang untuk memicu diskusi. Jadi, Anda mengatakan ada orang yang berkata, jika kita ingin membahas hal ini, mengapa memilih mereka yang dianggap paling buruk, dan mereka yang bukan orang Amerika atau berasal dari tempat lain?

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.en: And also that our Justice Department, our law enforcement entities have spent so many years, hundreds and thousands of dollars prosecuting. You know, these are very, very serious cases. Of course, the president -- it's up to the president. The president can decide this at any moment. But it really does speak to kind of what is the purpose -- you know, is our justice system truly free and fair for all? Is everyone open to this or only those --
paragraphs.cn: Dan Departemen Kehakiman serta lembaga penegak hukum kita telah menghabiskan bertahun-tahun dan ratusan juta dolar untuk menuntut. Anda tahu, ini adalah kasus yang sangat, sangat serius. Tentu saja, presiden -- itu terserah presiden. Presiden dapat memutuskan kapan saja. Tapi ini benar-benar menunjukkan tujuan -- Anda tahu, apakah sistem peradilan kita benar-benar adil bagi semua orang? Apakah setiap orang memiliki kesempatan ini, atau hanya orang-orang tertentu --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, I see Zolan nodding up a storm. I know the pardon thing within the White House presidential beat, people following it, has been -- people have been talking about this for a long time, ever since the sort of mass pardon that also included January 6th.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, saya melihat Zolan mengangguk setuju. Saya tahu masalah pengampunan dalam liputan presiden Gedung Putih, orang-orang yang memperhatikannya -- orang-orang sudah membicarakan ini sejak pengampunan massal yang mencakup 6 Januari.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But it's been a long conversation about who gets a pardon and how that happens.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi soal siapa yang mendapatkan pengampunan dan bagaimana cara mendapatkannya, sudah menjadi diskusi panjang.

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: Who gets a pardon. That's the key, I think, here too, how that happens. It's worth reminding folks that there is a process in place for this. A process that --
paragraphs.cn: Siapa yang mendapat pengampunan. Saya pikir itu juga kuncinya, dan bagaimana cara mendapatkannya. Perlu diingat, ada prosedur yang sudah ditetapkan. Sebuah prosedur --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The process is not just calling him or his family?
paragraphs.cn: Prosedur itu bukan hanya menelepon dia atau keluarganya?

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: No. No. Or not -- in a pre-Trump 2.0 sort of era, right, you have a situation where there's a long backlog for the clemency process, referrals made from experts, and then it's being processed through the Justice Department as well. The Office of the Pardon Attorney usually handling this as well. That has been thrown out of the door.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, tidak, atau bukan -- sebelum era Trump 2.0, kan, Anda akan menemukan situasi di mana proses pengampunan sudah lama tertunda, para ahli memberikan rekomendasi, dan rekomendasi itu juga diproses melalui Departemen Kehakiman. Kantor Jaksa Pengampunan biasanya juga menangani hal ini. Tapi sekarang semua itu ditinggalkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.en: And here you have a system where there's almost a cottage industry that's been created, where lobbyists are jockeying with other allies of the president trying to win favor to get people pardoned.
paragraphs.cn: Dan sekarang, di sini hampir terbentuk sistem rumahan, di mana para pelobi bersaing dengan sekutu presiden lainnya untuk menyenangkan hati, mencoba memenangkan kesempatan untuk mengampuni orang lain.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But, Sarah, can I ask, this is the kind of story where at some point someone will say, well, what about? What about Biden? What about Clinton? What about Obama? There's a lot of whataboutism when it comes to White House pardons. So, to you, what about that? Is this all that different or is this one of those Trump things where it's not different it's just out loud?
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Tapi, Sarah, bolehkah saya bertanya, cerita seperti ini pasti akan ada yang bilang, bagaimana dengan Biden? Clinton? Obama? Ada banyak argumen 'bagaimana dengan' dalam soal pengampunan Gedung Putih. Jadi, bagi Anda, apa bedanya? Atau ini hanya masalah lain dari Trump, sebenarnya tidak ada bedanya, hanya diucapkan dengan lantang?

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.en: I think what's different here is that there are people who are making money off of this in a -- at a mass --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir perbedaannya adalah ada orang yang mengambil keuntungan besar dari ini --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: People make money off of helping --
paragraphs.cn: Orang-orang mengambil keuntungan dengan membantu orang lain --

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.en: And it's not just, you know, this is an example of, I think, a leadership vacuum or the mixed messaging that comes out of this White House. For example, this is a frenzy. I cannot tell you. I have lawyers and lobbyists who are telling me, I'm so exhausted I have to turn off my phone because everyone thinks that this is an option for them if they pay enough money.
paragraphs.cn: Dan ini bukan hanya, Anda tahu, saya pikir ini adalah contoh kekosongan kepemimpinan atau Gedung Putih yang menyampaikan pesan kacau. Misalnya, ini benar-benar hiruk-pikuk. Saya tidak bisa menggambarkannya. Ada pengacara dan pelobi yang mengatakan kepada saya bahwa mereka sangat lelah sampai harus mematikan ponsel, karena semua orang berpikir asal bayar cukup, ini adalah pilihan.

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.en: And the White House came out with a statement that was very interesting in response when we came to them with this reporting and said, the president, you know, thinks it's detestable that anyone would try and profit off this. But that's not the messaging that's coming from the president's closest aides. And they could, you know, in the Biden administration, we did see efforts to say, like, knock it off. Everything goes through DOJ. We are going to keep this very close and very tight. That's not the message here.
paragraphs.cn: Dan Gedung Putih mengeluarkan pernyataan yang sangat menarik setelah kami melaporkan ini, mengatakan bahwa presiden menganggap siapa pun yang mencoba mengambil keuntungan dari ini adalah hina. Tapi itu bukan pesan yang disampaikan oleh para pembantu terdekat presiden. Dan, Anda tahu, di era pemerintahan Biden, kami memang melihat upaya untuk mengatakan 'jangan main-main'. Semuanya diproses melalui Departemen Kehakiman. Kami akan mengendalikan ini dengan sangat ketat. Bukan pesan itu yang disampaikan di sini.

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.en: The message here is, when you come to the White House, hey, if you've got a client that might be interested, let us know. And so, I think that, you know, we're seeing the opportunities for real corruption happening at a massive scale.
paragraphs.cn: Pesan yang disampaikan di sini adalah, ketika Anda datang ke Gedung Putih, hei, jika Anda memiliki klien yang mungkin tertarik, beri tahu kami. Jadi, saya pikir, Anda tahu, kita sedang melihat peluang korupsi besar-besaran terjadi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right. And it only takes one of those names to come out that it goes sideways for it to blow back hard and backfire with the public. I want to turn to this because we're talking about this idea of pardons. Earlier, we were talking about what's happening in Congress. But what's not happening in Congress is legislating. Members of the House have gone home. They have no clear path for passing a list of very key bills.
paragraphs.cn: Tepat. Dan hanya perlu satu nama saja yang terungkap, maka semuanya akan menjadi buruk, memicu reaksi publik yang besar. Saya ingin beralih ke topik ini, karena kita sedang membahas ide pengampunan. Sebelumnya, kita membahas apa yang terjadi di Kongres. Tapi yang tidak dilakukan Kongres adalah membuat undang-undang. Anggota DPR sudah pulang. Mereka tidak memiliki jalur yang jelas untuk mengesahkan serangkaian RUU yang sangat penting.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And here's the thing, hard line conservatives have refused to support some of these bills because they're frustrated with the president's lead issue, the SAVE America Act, his voting regulation act. President Trump has made this a priority, but there are divisions. Obviously, Senate Republicans say they don't have the votes to pass it. REP. HAKEEM JEFFRIES (D-NY): Mike Johnson and Steve Scalise have totally lost control of the House floor and are in the midst of a Republican civil war.
paragraphs.cn: Masalahnya, kaum konservatif garis keras menolak mendukung beberapa RUU ini karena mereka kecewa dengan agenda utama presiden, yaitu 'Save America Act' -- RUU pengawasan pemungutan suaranya -- yang menjadi prioritas Presiden Trump, tetapi ada perbedaan pendapat. Jelas, senator Partai Republik mengatakan mereka tidak memiliki cukup suara untuk meloloskannya. Anggota DPR Hakeem Jeffries (Demokrat dari New York): Mike Johnson dan Steve Scalise benar-benar kehilangan kendali atas agenda DPR, dan sedang dalam perang saudara Partai Republik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Donald Trump is fighting with Senate Republicans. Senate Republicans are fighting with House Republicans. And House Republicans are fighting with each other. The entire thing is a mess. Meanwhile, House Democrats are continuing to fight to make life better for the American people.
paragraphs.cn: Donald Trump sedang bertengkar dengan senator Partai Republik. Senator Partai Republik bertengkar dengan anggota DPR Partai Republik. Dan anggota DPR Partai Republik saling bertengkar satu sama lain. Seluruh situasi berantakan. Sementara itu, anggota DPR Partai Demokrat terus berjuang untuk memperbaiki kehidupan rakyat Amerika.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Shades on emoji. He's not wrong, right? They basically had to torpedo this vote because people are so upset about the SAVE Act not moving forward. They had to figure out a way to get it off the floor quickly. You're nodding. Have you been following this as well?
paragraphs.cn: Emoji kacamata hitam. Dia benar, kan? Mereka pada dasarnya harus menggagalkan pemungutan suara ini karena orang-orang sangat tidak puas dengan RUU Penyelamatan yang tidak bisa maju. Mereka harus mencari cara untuk segera menariknya dari agenda. Kamu mengangguk. Kamu juga mengikuti ini?

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.en: I mean it's chaos. It's a level of chaos and it shows a level of kind of lack of message discipline in which, you know, we're not seeing actual -- Congress do its actual job, but people are really frustrated.
paragraphs.cn: Maksudku, ini sangat kacau. Tingkat kekacauan ini menunjukkan kurangnya disiplin informasi, kita tidak melihat Kongres benar-benar menjalankan tugasnya, tetapi orang-orang memang merasa sangat frustrasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But it's like the president wants this. I was reading a quote from Tim Burchett, a Republican, and he said, you know what? You guys are complaining about us being foils. We're in lock step with the White House, he said. So, is this a way of Trump, who ran into a brick wall in the Senate over lunch, finding a way to get the SAVE Act back?
paragraphs.cn: Tapi Presiden menginginkannya. Saya membaca pernyataan dari anggota Partai Republik Tim Burchett, dia berkata, Anda tahu? Anda mengeluh kami menjadi penghalang. Kami sejalan dengan Gedung Putih. Jadi, apakah ini cara Trump untuk menemukan cara agar RUU Penyelamatan kembali ke jalur yang benar setelah ditolak di Senat saat makan siang?

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: I think the Senate's the real problem here. I mean this -- we have -- we are so far away from the 1980s where --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir Senat adalah masalah sebenarnya. Maksudku, kita sudah terlalu jauh dari tahun 1980-an, ketika -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But is the answer is, he gets -- he gets a no there and so now it's like everyone in --
paragraphs.cn: Tapi jawabannya bukan, dia ditolak di sana, jadi sekarang semua orang -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Well, what can he do?
paragraphs.cn: Lalu, apa yang bisa dia lakukan?

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Schilling
paragraphs.en: At this point we have the SAVE Act, which has five provisions that have 80 to 85 percent support amongst the American people. If you can't get that through the Senate, what can you get through? I don't know, spending trillions more dollars?
paragraphs.cn: Saat ini kita memiliki RUU Penyelamatan, yang lima ketentuannya didukung oleh 80% hingga 85% rakyat Amerika. Jika ini pun tidak bisa lolos di Senat, lalu apa yang bisa? Saya tidak tahu, menambah pengeluaran triliunan dolar lagi?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, what you can't do then is talk about your affordability bill. You can't pass a defense bill. You can't do -- how -- like, there's so much stuff that you can't do because you're busy fighting for this particular thing.
paragraphs.cn: Maka, Anda tidak bisa membicarakan RUU keterjangkauan Anda. Anda tidak bisa mengesahkan RUU pertahanan. Anda tidak bisa melakukan -- -- banyak hal yang tidak bisa Anda lakukan karena Anda sibuk berjuang untuk hal khusus ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: The least productive Congress in the history of this country. And the only thing --
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah Kongres paling tidak efisien dalam sejarah Amerika. Satu-satunya hal -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Thank you, Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: Terima kasih, Partai Demokrat.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: The only thing we do in politics is make the comparison. You think about when Democrats were in charge, the most productive Congress we've had in generations, including bipartisan legislation that actually helped the American people.
paragraphs.cn: Satu-satunya hal yang kami lakukan dalam politik adalah perbandingan. Pikirkan ketika Partai Demokrat berkuasa, itu adalah Kongres paling produktif dalam beberapa generasi, termasuk undang-undang bipartisan yang benar-benar membantu rakyat Amerika.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Productive (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: Produktif (tidak jelas).

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: That's why Donald Trump is going to be your biggest liability in November.
paragraphs.cn: Itulah mengapa Donald Trump akan menjadi beban terbesar Anda pada bulan November.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Hey, like press, there's no such thing as bad production. OK, Terry, that name of the game is producing, and that's why we're ending with you because., my man, I hear you have news in your group chat.
paragraphs.cn: Hei, teman-teman media, tidak ada yang namanya produksi yang buruk. Oke, Terry, nama permainannya adalah produksi, jadi kami akhiri denganmu karena, kawan, saya dengar ada berita di grup chatmu.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Oh my gosh. Yes. So, my friends and I, my daughter just got engaged. She's 20. She's getting married next summer. And my friends all pointed out that I'm going to be the first in our friend group, most likely, to be a grandfather.
paragraphs.cn: Ya ampun. Ya. Jadi, saya dan teman-teman saya, putri saya baru saja bertunangan. Dia 20 tahun. Dia akan menikah musim panas depan. Teman-teman saya menunjukkan bahwa saya kemungkinan akan menjadi orang pertama di antara teman-teman kami yang menjadi kakek.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Might be the first in this table, Terry.
paragraphs.cn: Terry, mungkin yang pertama di meja ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: (INAUDIBLE). How old?
paragraphs.cn: (Tidak jelas) berapa umur?

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: I'm 39. I'll be 40 this month.
paragraphs.cn: Saya 39 tahun. Bulan ini akan 40.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: I am here for it. Look --
paragraphs.cn: Saya siap. Lihat -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Drop the skin care for the chat.
paragraphs.cn: Tinggalkan perawatan kulit, ayo ngobrol.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: There's so much economic uncertainty right now. And like, what my daughter has figured out is like, you just find that one that you can be a partner with and you take on the world together and you'll figure it out. And so, I'm very happy. Congratulations. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang ketidakpastian ekonomi terlalu besar. Yang dipahami putri saya adalah, Anda hanya perlu menemukan seseorang yang bisa menjadi pasangan Anda, menghadapi dunia bersama, dan Anda bisa menyelesaikan semuanya. Jadi, saya senang. Selamat. (Percakapan silang)

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's funny, we were talking about this on the podcast because in the world of youth, Christian college culture, there's ring by spring, which is to try and get married before you leave campus and go out into the wide world where you will become, and I quote, "a hinge widow." Thank you all for being with me. These headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Menariknya, kami juga membahas ini di podcast, karena di dunia anak muda, budaya universitas Kristen memiliki istilah 'cincin musim semi', yaitu mencoba menikah sebelum meninggalkan kampus dan memasuki dunia luas, dan Anda akan menjadi, saya kutip, 'janda engsel'. Terima kasih semuanya. Selanjutnya berita utama.

vocabulary.en: the group of people in a society or organization who have power and influence and do not want change

vocabulary.en: favoring or implementing social reform or new, liberal ideas
vocabulary.excerpt: do Democrats see progressives as a problem?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 民主党人是否将进步派视为问题？

vocabulary.en: a person who advocates for a political system combining democratic elections with socialist economic policies
vocabulary.excerpt: Another Democratic socialist knocks out a moderate.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 又一位民主社会主义者击败了一名温和派。

vocabulary.en: a person with moderate views, especially in politics
vocabulary.excerpt: Another Democratic socialist knocks out a moderate.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 又一位民主社会主义者击败了一名温和派。

vocabulary.en: a preliminary election to appoint delegates to a party conference or to select the candidates for a principal election
vocabulary.excerpt: Stunning losses in Colorado. State attorney general Phil Weiser upsets Senator Michael Bennet in the Democratic primary for governor.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 科罗拉多州出现惊人失利。州总检察长菲尔·韦泽在民主党州长初选中击败参议员迈克尔·贝内特。

vocabulary.en: the current holder of an office or position
vocabulary.excerpt: Melat Kiros beat a nearly 30-year incumbent congresswoman, Diana DeGette
vocabulary.exampleCn: 梅拉特·基罗斯击败了连任近30年的现任众议员戴安娜·德盖特

vocabulary.en: to defeat an opponent that is expected to win
vocabulary.excerpt: State attorney general Phil Weiser upsets Senator Michael Bennet
vocabulary.exampleCn: 州总检察长菲尔·韦泽击败参议员迈克尔·贝内特

vocabulary.en: an expression of sharp disapproval or criticism
vocabulary.excerpt: A rare rebuke from the conservative court. Birthright citizenship upheld.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 来自保守派法院的罕见谴责。出生公民权得到维持。

vocabulary.cn: 出生公民权（在美国出生即自动获得公民身份）
vocabulary.en: the right of anyone born in a country to be a citizen of that country
vocabulary.excerpt: Birthright citizenship upheld. How the president wants to get his way anyway.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 出生公民权得到维持。总统无论如何都想达成目的。

vocabulary.en: to confirm or support a decision, especially a legal one
vocabulary.excerpt: Supreme Court Upholds Bans on Transgender Athletes.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 最高法院维持对跨性别运动员的禁令。

vocabulary.en: denoting or relating to a person whose gender identity does not correspond with the sex registered at birth
vocabulary.excerpt: Supreme Court Upholds Bans on Transgender Athletes.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 最高法院维持对跨性别运动员的禁令。

vocabulary.en: a large, destructive fire that spreads quickly over woodland or brush
vocabulary.excerpt: Western wildfires explode, scorching thousands of acres.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 西部野火爆发，烧毁数千英亩。

vocabulary.en: to burn the surface of something with intense heat
vocabulary.excerpt: Western wildfires explode, scorching thousands of acres.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 西部野火爆发，烧毁数千英亩。

vocabulary.cn: 自由球员（合同到期可自由签约的运动员）
vocabulary.en: a professional athlete who is not under contract and is free to sign with any team
vocabulary.excerpt: LeBron James, a free agent. And almost every NBA team is sure to want him.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 勒布朗·詹姆斯成为自由球员。几乎每支NBA球队都肯定想要他。

vocabulary.en: a failure or disappointment
vocabulary.excerpt: In Lakers' standards, it was -- it was a bust. It was a waste of money.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 按照湖人的标准，那是一次失败。那是浪费钱。

vocabulary.en: a small group of people having control of a country or organization
vocabulary.excerpt: They said the establishment and the oligarchy is just too big and too powerful to overcome.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们说建制派和寡头集团太大、太强大，无法战胜。

vocabulary.cn: 有组织的人民 vs. 有组织的金钱（政治口号）
vocabulary.en: a phrase contrasting grassroots mobilization with financial power
vocabulary.excerpt: You are the proof that the power of organized people beats the power of organized money.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 你们就是证明：有组织的人民的力量战胜有组织的金钱的力量。

vocabulary.en: to succeed in overcoming an obstacle or limit
vocabulary.excerpt: We broke through a lot of negative ads. We broke through a lot of noise.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们突破了许多负面广告。我们突破了许多噪音。

vocabulary.en: conflict within a group or organization
vocabulary.excerpt: back a few months ago when there was infighting in the DNC itself
vocabulary.exampleCn: 几个月前，当民主党全国委员会内部出现内斗时

vocabulary.cn: 民主党全国委员会 (Democratic National Committee)
vocabulary.en: the governing body of the United States Democratic Party
vocabulary.excerpt: back a few months ago when there was infighting in the DNC itself
vocabulary.exampleCn: 几个月前，当民主党全国委员会内部出现内斗时

vocabulary.en: to evoke or suggest images, memories, or emotions
vocabulary.excerpt: I do think the themes in these races speak to -- they resonate with something broader in the Democratic base across the country.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我确实认为这些竞选的主题与全国民主党基础选民中更广泛的东西产生了共鸣。

vocabulary.cn: 代际竞选（年轻挑战者与年长在位者之间的竞选）
vocabulary.en: an election contest between candidates of different generations
vocabulary.excerpt: You just highlighted somebody who won a generational race, right?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 你刚刚强调了一位赢得代际竞选的候选人，对吧？

vocabulary.en: the deliberate killing of a large number of people from a particular nation or ethnic group
vocabulary.excerpt: We will not wait to put an end to the politics of the past; to get big money out of our politics; and to reject corporate PACs and AIPAC. And no, we will not wait to end the genocide in Palestine.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们不会等待结束过去的政治；将大笔资金赶出我们的政治；拒绝企业政治行动委员会和AIPAC。不，我们不会等待结束巴勒斯坦的种族灭绝。

vocabulary.cn: 美国以色列公共事务委员会 (American Israel Public Affairs Committee)
vocabulary.en: a pro-Israel lobbying group in the United States
vocabulary.excerpt: to reject corporate PACs and AIPAC.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 拒绝企业政治行动委员会和AIPAC。

sentences.en: And what we're seeing with this series of wins, the ones you just outlined, but also were coming a week after Mamdani's sweep in New York.
sentences.cn: 我们看到的这一系列胜利，不仅是你刚才提到的那些，而且是在曼达尼在纽约大获全胜一周后出现的。
sentences.structure: 主句为 what we're seeing...，后接同位语 the ones you just outlined，but also 引导并列结构，were coming 为谓语，a week after... 为时间状语。
sentences.analysis: what 引导主语从句；同位语 the ones 补充说明 wins；but also 连接并列谓语；sweep 为新闻常用词，指压倒性胜利。

sentences.en: I do think the themes in these races speak to -- they resonate with something broader in the Democratic base across the country.
sentences.cn: 我确实认为这些竞选的主题反映了 -- -- 它们与全国民主党基础中更广泛的东西产生共鸣。
sentences.structure: 主句 I do think，后接宾语从句 the themes...speak to，破折号后为补充说明的同位语从句 they resonate with...。
sentences.analysis: do 强调谓语；speak to 意为"反映，表明"；resonate with 意为"与……共鸣"；broader 为比较级，强调范围更广。

sentences.en: In the last presidential campaign, I would often hear from Democratic voters they didn't feel people represented them.
sentences.cn: 在上次总统竞选中，我经常听到民主党选民说，他们觉得没有人代表他们。
sentences.structure: 时间状语 In the last presidential campaign 前置，主句 I would often hear from Democratic voters，后接省略 that 的宾语从句 they didn't feel people represented them。
sentences.analysis: would 表示过去习惯性动作；hear from 意为"收到……的来信/消息"；feel 后接宾语从句，省略 that；represented 为过去式。

sentences.en: She was born a year after the incumbent won her first race.
sentences.cn: 她是在现任议员赢得第一次竞选一年后出生的。
sentences.structure: 主句 She was born，后接时间状语从句 a year after the incumbent won her first race。
sentences.analysis: after 引导时间状语从句；incumbent 指现任议员；race 在此指竞选。

sentences.en: And the issue of whether the Democratic Party will support U.S. support for Israel, that has resonated both in New York and these races.
sentences.cn: 而民主党是否会支持美国对以色列的支持这一问题，在纽约和这些竞选中都引起了共鸣。
sentences.structure: 主语 the issue of whether...，后接同位语从句 whether the Democratic Party will support...，主句谓语 has resonated，宾语 both in New York and these races。
sentences.analysis: whether 引导同位语从句；support 出现两次，注意区分：第一个 support 为动词，第二个为名词；resonate 意为"引起共鸣"。

sentences.en: I think what happened with Kamala Harris and Biden is they truly struggled to talk about Israel and Gaza when that fight was at its height.
sentences.cn: 我认为卡玛拉·哈里斯和拜登的情况是，当那场战斗最激烈时，他们确实很难谈论以色列和加沙。
sentences.structure: 主句 I think，后接宾语从句 what happened...is they truly struggled...，其中 what 引导主语从句，is 后为表语从句（省略 that），when 引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: what 引导主语从句；struggled to do 意为"努力做某事，艰难地做"；at its height 意为"在最激烈/最高峰时"。

sentences.en: When I wrote a letter defending students' rights to protest the genocide in Gaza, my law firm told me, Take it down or you're fired.
sentences.cn: 当我写一封捍卫学生抗议加沙种族灭绝权利的信时，我的律师事务所告诉我，要么撤下它，要么你被解雇。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句 When I wrote...，主句 my law firm told me，后接直接引语 Take it down or you're fired。
sentences.analysis: defending 为现在分词作定语修饰 letter；genocide 意为"种族灭绝"；Take it down or you're fired 为祈使句+并列句，表示威胁。

sentences.en: We will not wait to put an end to the politics of the past; to get big money out of our politics; and to reject corporate PACs and AIPAC.
sentences.cn: 我们不会等待结束过去的政治；将大笔资金从我们的政治中剔除；并拒绝企业政治行动委员会和AIPAC。
sentences.structure: 主句 We will not wait，后接三个并列的不定式短语 to put an end to...; to get...; and to reject...。
sentences.analysis: wait to do 意为"等待做某事"；put an end to 意为"结束"；get...out of 意为"把……从……中剔除"；corporate PACs 指企业政治行动委员会；AIPAC 为美国以色列公共事务委员会。

sentences.en: And no, we will not wait to end the genocide in Palestine.
sentences.cn: 不，我们不会等待结束巴勒斯坦的种族灭绝。
sentences.structure: 插入语 And no 后接主句 we will not wait to end...。
sentences.analysis: no 为独立成分，表示强调否定；wait to end 意为"等待结束"；genocide 再次出现，强调立场。

sentences.en: I think the common denominator is that people are frustrated.
sentences.cn: 我认为共同点是人们感到沮丧。
sentences.structure: 主句 I think，后接宾语从句 the common denominator is that...，其中 that 引导表语从句。
sentences.analysis: common denominator 原为数学术语"公分母"，此处比喻"共同点"；frustrated 意为"沮丧的"。

sentences.en: You can talk boldly and loudly about all of these policies, but if you cannot govern, if you do not win, and losers do not legislate.
sentences.cn: 你可以大胆而响亮地谈论所有这些政策，但如果你不能执政，如果你不赢，而失败者不能立法。
sentences.structure: 并列句：前半句 You can talk...，but 后为条件状语从句 if you cannot govern, if you do not win, and losers do not legislate。
sentences.analysis: boldly and loudly 为并列副词修饰 talk；govern 意为"执政"；legislate 意为"立法"；注意最后一句为独立分句，与前面 if 从句并列。

sentences.en: And when you talk about the way that some of our more progressive candidates who are winning in deep-blue districts, the issues they are talking about are going to require them to have to compromise on what they believe if they want to respond to what they campaign.
sentences.cn: 当你谈到我们一些更进步的候选人在深蓝选区获胜的方式时，他们谈论的问题将要求他们必须在自己信仰的事情上妥协，如果他们想回应他们竞选时所承诺的。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句 when you talk about the way...，其中 way 后接 that 引导的定语从句，定语从句中嵌套 who 引导的定语从句修饰 candidates；主句 the issues...are going to require them to have to compromise on what they believe，后接 if 条件状语从句，其中 what they campaign 为宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: deep-blue districts 指民主党占绝对优势的选区；require sb. to do 意为"要求某人做某事"；compromise on 意为"在……上妥协"；respond to 意为"回应"；campaign 在此为动词，意为"竞选，宣传"。

sentences.en: I think we've put these labels on folks, and I think that has been part of the somewhat division you see within the primaries.
sentences.cn: 我认为我们给人们贴上了这些标签，而且我认为这已经成为你在初选中看到的那种分裂的一部分。
sentences.structure: 并列句：I think we've put...，and I think that has been...，其中 that 指代前句内容，you see within the primaries 为省略 that 的定语从句修饰 division。
sentences.analysis: put labels on 意为"给……贴标签"；somewhat 为副词，修饰 division，意为"有点，某种程度上的"；primaries 指初选。

sentences.en: At the end of the day, when you go into the United States Congress, you're labeled as a Democrat versus Republican.
sentences.cn: 归根结底，当你进入美国国会时，你被贴上民主党或共和党的标签。
sentences.structure: 时间状语 At the end of the day 和 when you go into...，主句 you're labeled as...。
sentences.analysis: At the end of the day 为固定短语，意为"最终，说到底"；labeled as 意为"被贴上……的标签"；versus 意为"对，与……相对"。

sentences.en: I think this is probably true in Republican primaries.
sentences.cn: 我认为这在共和党初选中可能也是如此。
sentences.structure: 主句 I think，后接宾语从句 this is probably true...。
sentences.analysis: probably 表示可能性；true 意为"真实的，正确的"；in Republican primaries 为地点状语。

sentences.en: It's easier to vote against something than it is to vote for something.
sentences.cn: 投票反对某件事比投票支持某件事更容易。
sentences.structure: 形式主语 It，真正主语 to vote against something，than 引导比较状语从句 it is to vote for something。
sentences.analysis: It's easier to do A than to do B 为比较结构；vote against 意为"投票反对"；vote for 意为"投票支持"。

sentences.en: And who they feel like a leader.
sentences.cn: 以及他们觉得谁是领导者。
sentences.structure: 省略主句的从句，完整应为 and (people are voting for) who they feel like a leader，其中 who 引导宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: feel like 在此意为"觉得是"；leader 意为"领导者"。

sentences.en: I'm looking at like, for instance, the "Post" response to the Supreme Court upholding bans on trans athletes.
sentences.cn: 比如，我正在看《华盛顿邮报》对最高法院维持跨性别运动员禁令的回应。
sentences.structure: 主句 I'm looking at，插入语 like, for instance，宾语 the "Post" response，后接介词短语 to the Supreme Court upholding bans on trans athletes。
sentences.analysis: look at 意为"看"；upholding 为动名词，作介词 to 的宾语，意为"维持，支持"；bans on 意为"对……的禁令"；trans 为 transgender 的缩写。

sentences.en: This kind of follows what he's saying.
sentences.cn: 这有点符合他所说的。
sentences.structure: 主句 This kind of follows，后接宾语从句 what he's saying。
sentences.analysis: kind of 为口语表达，意为"有点，某种程度上"；follow 意为"遵循，符合"。

sentences.en: It's easy to say no.
sentences.cn: 说"不"很容易。
sentences.structure: 形式主语 It，真正主语 to say no。
sentences.analysis: It's easy to do 结构；say no 意为"说不，拒绝"。

topics.title: 民主党初选中的进步派崛起
topics.content: 2026年科罗拉多州民主党初选中，进步派候选人梅拉特·基罗斯（Melat Kiros）击败连任近30年的温和派众议员戴安娜·德盖特（Diana DeGette），州总检察长菲尔·韦泽（Phil Weiser）击败参议员迈克尔·贝内特（Michael Bennet）。这反映了民主党内部代际和意识形态分歧加剧，进步派通过反对建制派、反对AIPAC和以色列政策、主张全民医保等议题动员年轻选民。Justice Democrats等组织在多地初选中获胜，挑战传统民主党领导层。
topics.keywords: Democratic primary · progressive · establishment · Justice Democrats · AIPAC

topics.title: 最高法院维持跨性别运动员禁令
topics.content: 美国最高法院裁定维持对跨性别运动员参加女子体育比赛的禁令。这一裁决被视为保守派法院的立场体现，支持者认为保护了女子体育的公平性，反对者则批评其歧视跨性别群体。该议题成为文化战争焦点，影响2026年中期选举。
topics.keywords: Supreme Court · transgender athletes · ban · uphold · conservative court

topics.title: 出生公民权争议与特朗普盟友的挑战
topics.content: 最高法院维持了出生公民权（birthright citizenship），即在美国出生即自动获得公民身份的权利。特朗普及其盟友曾试图通过行政命令废除该权利，但遭遇法律障碍。此次裁决后，特朗普盟友誓言继续通过其他方式限制移民，包括推动立法或修改宪法解释。
topics.keywords: birthright citizenship · Trump allies · Supreme Court · immigration · executive order

topics.title: 西部野火与极端天气
topics.content: 美国西部多州爆发大规模野火，烧毁数千英亩土地。气象部门警告，本周后期可能出现更危险的天气条件，如强风和干燥雷暴，加剧火势。野火季节提前且强度增加，与气候变化相关，给消防和应急管理带来巨大压力。
topics.keywords: wildfire · Western US · scorch · extreme weather · climate change

quiz.question: 在原文 'Another Democratic socialist knocks out a moderate.' 中，'moderate' 最接近的含义是？
quiz.explanation: 在政治语境中，'moderate' 作为名词指政治立场温和的人，即温和派。原文中与 'Democratic socialist' 对比，指击败了一位温和派候选人。

quiz.question: 短语 'organized people beats the power of organized money' 中，'organized money' 指的是？
options: B. 大财团和利益集团的政治献金
quiz.explanation: 在政治语境中，'organized money' 指通过政治行动委员会（PACs）、企业捐赠等方式集中使用的资金，代表建制派和既得利益集团的力量。

quiz.question: 原文 'A rare rebuke from the conservative court.' 中，'rebuke' 的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'Rebuke' 意为严厉批评或谴责。这里指保守派占多数的最高法院罕见地批评了政府或某项政策，结合上下文是维持出生公民权，对特朗普政府构成打击。

quiz.question: 根据文章，梅拉特·基罗斯（Melat Kiros）在竞选中主要反对什么？
options: B. 以色列政策和AIPAC的影响
quiz.explanation: 基罗斯在胜选演讲中明确表示要拒绝企业PAC和AIPAC，并结束巴勒斯坦的种族灭绝，表明她反对以色列现行政策和AIPAC的游说影响力。

quiz.question: 分析人士认为进步派候选人在初选中获胜的主要原因是什么？
options: A. Mereka mendapatkan lebih banyak dukungan dana
B. Mereka mewakili ketidakpuasan pemilih terhadap kemapanan dan pergantian generasi
C. Mereka mendapat dukungan dari Partai Republik
D. Mereka fokus pada isu-isu lokal
quiz.explanation: 文章指出，选民感到被建制派忽视，进步派候选人通过反对金钱政治、以色列政策等议题，以及年轻一代的活力，赢得了对现状不满的选民支持。

quiz.question: Apa yang disiratkan artikel tentang keputusan Mahkamah Agung mengenai larangan atlet transgender?
options: B. Keputusan mencerminkan posisi pengadilan konservatif
D. Keputusan tidak terkait dengan kasus kewarganegaraan berdasarkan kelahiran
quiz.explanation: Artikel menyebut keputusan tersebut sebagai 'rare rebuke from the conservative court', menyiratkan bahwa mayoritas konservatif Mahkamah Agung membuat keputusan kritis yang tidak biasa, mempertahankan larangan, yang mencerminkan kecenderungan konservatifnya.
